features.name: integer index in for-loop with parameter-dependent vector width
features.description: Code should include a for-loop using an integer variable as index, where the loop bound WID equals 2^LG and exceeds 31 (causing integer index overflow behavior)

features.name: bit-selective index extraction from integer loop variable
features.description: Code should include an integer variable being indexed with a bit-select range N[LG-1:0] where LG controls the width and N is the full integer value

features.name: attribute instance before parameter declaration
features.description: Code should include an attribute instance placed before a parameter declaration

metadata.snippet: (* attr = 1 *) parameter P1 = 8;

features.name: attribute instance before localparam declaration
features.description: Code should include an attribute instance placed before a localparam declaration

metadata.snippet: (* attr = 1 *) localparam LP1 = 4;

features.name: bram initialization with subsequent write
features.description: Code should include BRAM (unpacked array of 512+ entries) with initial value written in an initial block, followed by a write to a different address in a clocked always block

features.name: bram read after init and write
features.description: Code should include a BRAM read from the initially-written address after a subsequent write operation to another address occurs

features.name: unsigned genvar descending loop with >=0 condition
features.description: Code should include a generate for loop where a genvar decrements and uses a >=0 termination condition, causing an infinite loop since genvar is unsigned and wraps below zero

features.name: duplicate parameter redeclaration in function
features.description: Code should include a function definition with an input parameter declared twice, first as a bit-width type and then redeclared as an integer type

features.name: unsized single-bit literal constant with apostrophe
features.description: Code should include an unsized single-bit literal constant using apostrophe notation without base specifier, such as '0, '1, 'x, or 'z, in an assignment or expression context

metadata.snippet:
logic [31:0] b;
assign b = '0;

features.name: shiftx cell with empty B port connection
features.description: Code should include a $shiftx cell with an empty port connection on the B port to trigger proc_clean misclassifying a port as unused wire

metadata.snippet: $shiftx #(.A_SIGNED(32'd0),.A_WIDTH(32'd1),.B_SIGNED(32'd0),.B_WIDTH(32'sd0),.Y_WIDTH(32'd1)) _34_(.A(i[4]),.B(),.Y(o));

features.name: cell with disconnected output port
features.description: Code should include a cell output port that is explicitly disconnected (empty connection) to trigger the proc_clean regression

metadata.snippet: module top(input [4:0] i, output o); wire unused; $shiftx _(.A(i[4]),.B(),.Y(o)); endmodule

wand E;

b b_inst (.D(D));

always @(posedge clk) begin q1 <= q0_next; end

metadata.snippet: block_a dut(.in('1), .out(out));

features.name: ROM memory with $readmemh initialization
features.description: Code should include a read-only memory initialized with $readmemh where the memory has no write port and is accessed via procedural continuous assignment

features.name: for loop memory initialization preserves memory cells
features.description: Code should include a read-only memory initialized with a for loop where the memory has no write port but memory cells are still generated

features.name: if statement with null statement body
features.description: Code should include an if statement with only a semicolon (null statement) as its body, without any statement block

metadata.snippet: if (on);

features.name: chained multiply_add without parentheses
features.description: Code should include a chained multiply-add expression where multiple products are summed without explicit parentheses grouping

features.name: signed_multiply with accumulation
features.description: Code should include signed 16-bit multiply operations whose results are accumulated into a wider bit-width register

features.name: multiplication result chained with addition
features.description: Code should include an expression where the result of one multiplication is directly added to the result of another multiplication without intermediate parentheses

features.name: reg with both always assignment and continuous assign
features.description: Code should include a signal declared as reg that is driven both inside an always block and via a continuous assign statement outside the always block

features.name: process with implicit default case
features.description: Code should include a case statement with an implicit default case (bare 'case' without explicit condition) inside a process

metadata.snippet:
process $group_1
  assign \a_pc 8'00000000
  switch \sel
    case 1'0
      assign \a_pc [7:2] 6'101010
    case
      assign \a_pc [7:2] 6'111111
  end
  sync init
end

features.name: attribute before null statement
features.description: Code should include an attribute placed before a null statement, using the syntax (* attribute_name *) followed by a semicolon

features.name: parameter_override_port_width
features.description: Code should include a parameterized module with port wire ranges using parameter expressions, instantiated with a parameter override that changes the width

features.name: part_select write with variable index in always @*
features.description: Code should include a part-select write to a register using a variable index expression inside a combinational always @* block

features.name: nowrshmsk attribute on register output
features.description: Code should include a register output with the nowrshmsk attribute set to 0 to trigger the loop detection issue

metadata.snippet: (* nowrshmsk = 0 *) output reg [1:0] x

features.name: constant wire used as index in part-select write
features.description: Code should include a constant zero wire used as part of an index expression for part-select writes in a for loop

features.name: task with untyped port declaration
features.description: Code should include a task declaration with a port that lacks type or size declaration

metadata.snippet:
task SomeTaskName(a)
endtask

features.name: multi-dimensional array with non-power-of-two elements
features.description: Code should include a multi-dimensional unpacked array declaration where the total element count is not a power of two

features.name: array initialization with default keyword
features.description: Code should include an array initialization using '{default:...}' syntax

metadata.snippet: mem[I][K] <= '{default:0};

features.name: variable index access to multi-dimensional array
features.description: Code should include multi-dimensional array access using variables as indices

metadata.snippet: dout <= mem[A][B][C];

features.name: replicated operand in adder for carry chain
features.description: Code should include an addition where one operand uses the replication operator to expand a single bit to match the adder width, creating a conditional increment pattern

metadata.snippet: cnt + { (WIDTH+1){last_n} }

features.name: ternary conditional with counter increment
features.description: Code should include a ternary conditional operator selecting between a counter increment and a constant literal, used in a registered output assignment

metadata.snippet: cnt <= last_n ? ( cnt + { (WIDTH+1){last_n} } ) : 13'h1aaa;

features.name: parameterized width counter with carry chain
features.description: Code should include a parameterized width counter where the MSB feeds back through a wire to control increment behavior, creating a dependency on carry chain logic

features.name: zero_width_positive_unary_operator
features.description: Code should include a $pos unary operator applied to a zero-width signal operand (A_WIDTH=0) during ECP5 or Xilinx synthesis flow

metadata.snippet: $pos zero_width_signal

features.name: readmem finish_addr premature break
features.description: Code should include a $readmemh or $readmem function call with an address range where finish_addr is the last address to be written, causing the last address's data to not be written due to premature loop break at cursor == finish_addr

metadata.snippet: $readmemh("rom.hex", 0, 3);

features.name: function_call_as_array_index_in_assign
features.description: Code should include a function call used as the index expression in an unpacked array assignment within a continuous assign statement

features.name: escaped_identifier_cell_name_collision_after_flatten
features.description: Code should include a module instantiation with an escaped cell name that could collide with cells from the instantiated module after flattening

features.name: hierarchy_flatten_with_escaped_cell_identifiers
features.description: Code should include a module containing cells with backslash-prefixed names that could conflict with cells from child modules during flatten

features.name: package enum typedef in interface
features.description: Code should include a typedef enum defined in a package and then used to declare a variable inside a SystemVerilog interface with pkg::type_name syntax

features.name: fsm_with_async_reset_adff
features.description: Code should include a finite state machine using async reset D flip-flops ($adff cells) with a state register and multiple control inputs/outputs

features.name: fsm_with_part_select_ctrl_outputs
features.description: Code should include FSM control outputs using bit-indexed part-select notation where the FSM extractor needs to construct identifiers containing [n] subscripts

features.name: fsm_transition_table_with_dont_care
features.description: Code should include FSM transition logic with don't-care bits in case statements or conditional expressions that create ambiguous state transitions

features.name: constant_function_in_generate_if_condition
features.description: Code should include a generate if construct where a constant function is called directly in the condition expression

features.name: ROM memory with non-power-of-2 word count and address boundary check
features.description: Code should include a ROM/memory with address width larger than the actual number of words, combined with an out-of-bounds check on the address or data output before using the value

features.name: asynchronous ROM with conditional output based on address range
features.description: Code should include an asynchronous read-only memory where the output is conditionally selected based on whether the address exceeds a threshold

metadata.snippet:
reg [11:0] rom [0:10];
wire [11:0] data = (addr >= 4'd12) ? {12{1'bx}} : rom[addr];

features.name: arithmetic right shift on narrow signed signal
features.description: Code should include an arithmetic right shift (>>>) applied to a signed signal whose bit-width is strictly less than 32 bits, so that the cxxrtl chunk-level sign-extension path is exercised

features.name: signed right shift result sign-extended into wider storage
features.description: Code should include an arithmetic right shift of a narrow signed value (< 32 bits) where the result is stored in a wider target, making incorrect sign-extension observable

features.name: signed shift by variable amount on sub-word signal
features.description: Code should include a signed right shift where the shift amount is a runtime variable and the operand is a signed signal narrower than the native word size (16 bits), stressing the final-chunk sign mask calculation

features.name: multiple independent clock domains in always blocks
features.description: Code should include two separate always @(posedge clk) blocks with different clock signals, each driving a separate register

metadata.snippet:
always @(posedge clk1) count1 <= count1 + 1'b1;
always @(posedge clk2) count2 <= count2 + 1'b1;

features.name: inout_port_partial_assignment
features.description: Code should include a module with inout ports where the module makes partial bit-range assignments to the inout signals (e.g., assign data_out[7:0] = data_in[7:0])

features.name: inout_port_instantiation_with_wire
features.description: Code should include a module instantiation where an inout port is connected to a wire, and the wire is also used in other assign statements

features.name: bidirectional_signal_split_assignment
features.description: Code should include bidirectional signals where different bits are assigned in different directions (some bits driven by input, others fed back from output)

metadata.snippet:
assign data_out[7:0] = data_in[7:0];
assign data_in[8] = data_out[8];

features.name: wildcard package import at module scope
features.description: Code should include a wildcard import statement from a package at module scope using 'import pkg_name::*;' syntax

features.name: comma-separated continuous assignments with wire chaining
features.description: Code should include comma-separated continuous assignments where one wire is assigned from an expression and another wire is assigned from the first wire in a chain

metadata.snippet:
wire s1;
assign s1 = ~a, z = s1;

features.name: shift cell with signed A operand
features.description: Code should include a $shift cell with A_SIGNED parameter set to 1, where the A operand has a negative value when interpreted as a signed number

metadata.snippet:
cell $shift UUT_shift
  parameter \A_SIGNED 1
  parameter \A_WIDTH 4
  parameter \B_WIDTH 4
  parameter \Y_WIDTH 8
  connect \A 4'1010
  connect \B 4'0001
  connect \Y \Y

features.name: comparison of $shift vs $shr with A_SIGNED
features.description: Code should include both $shift and $shr cells with identical parameters including A_SIGNED=1, where they produce different results due to $shift ignoring the sign extension

metadata.snippet:
cell $shift UUT_shift
  parameter \A_SIGNED 1
  parameter \A_WIDTH 4
  parameter \Y_WIDTH 8
  connect \A 4'1010
  connect \Y \Y_shift

cell $shr UUT_shr
  parameter \A_SIGNED 1
  parameter \A_WIDTH 4
  parameter \Y_WIDTH 8
  connect \A 4'1010
  connect \Y \Y_shr

features.name: $shiftx cell with A_SIGNED parameter
features.description: Code should include a $shiftx cell with A_SIGNED parameter set to 1, where the sign extension is ignored similar to $shift

metadata.snippet:
cell $shiftx UUT_shiftx
  parameter \A_SIGNED 1
  parameter \A_WIDTH 4
  parameter \B_WIDTH 4
  parameter \Y_WIDTH 8
  connect \A 4'1010
  connect \B 4'0001
  connect \Y \Y_shiftx

features.name: FSM with localparam state encoding and case-based next-state logic
features.description: Code should include an FSM using localparam constants for state encoding with a combinational always block using a case statement to derive next-state logic and a clocked always block for state register update

features.name: reduction AND operator applied to concatenation
features.description: Code should include a wire driven by a reduction AND operator applied to a multi-bit concatenation of scalar signals

features.name: formal assume constraint in combinational always block
features.description: Code should include a formal assume statement inside a combinational always @(*) block to constrain a signal to a constant value

metadata.snippet: always @(*) assume (rstn == 1'b1);

features.name: formal assert with precondition in clocked always block
features.description: Code should include a formal assert statement with an if-precondition inside a clocked always @(posedge clk) block

features.name: SVA assert property with disable iff and implication operator
features.description: Code should include an SVA assert property statement using a clocking event, disable iff guard, and the |-> implication operator to check a precondition-to-condition relationship

features.name: formal checker module encapsulating assertions with module instantiation
features.description: Code should include a dedicated checker module containing formal assume and assert statements that is instantiated into the DUT to encapsulate verification logic

features.name: genvar declared in named generate block shadowing same-named procedural locals
features.description: Code should include a named generate block that contains both named procedural blocks (initial, always) each declaring a local variable with the same identifier, and also declares a genvar with that same identifier used in a generate for-loop

features.name: relative hierarchical path resolution across sibling named generate sub-blocks
features.description: Code should include nested named generate blocks where a wire in a parent named block references signals in sibling sub-blocks using relative hierarchical paths (e.g. B.y instead of A.B.y)

features.name: multiple named procedural blocks inside a named generate block with shared variable names
features.description: Code should include a named generate block containing both a named initial block and a named always block, each declaring a local signed reg with the same identifier and assigning to an outer reg output

features.name: bitwise OR between signals of different widths
features.description: Code should include a bitwise OR operation between two logic signals of different bit widths, where the narrower operand is implicitly extended before the OR is applied, producing an $or cell with mismatched port widths in RTLIL

features.name: bit_select_assignment_with_variable_index_in_always_block
features.description: Code should include a bit-select assignment on a reg array element using a variable index within an always block, combined with unpacked array access using loop variable

features.name: DFF with x init legalized to ECP5
features.description: Code should include a D flip-flop (DFF) with an initial value of 1'x that gets legalized for ECP5 architecture, causing a conflict when the tool tries to set a concrete init value

features.name: DFF legalization conflicting init values
features.description: Code should include a scenario where DFFLEGALIZE pass encounters a signal with conflicting init values between 1'x and 1'0, which cannot be resolved for the target architecture

metadata.snippet:
reg [2:0] interface2_bank_bus_dat_r = 3'bx;
// DFFLEGALIZE for ECP5 cannot resolve 1'x vs 1'0 conflict

features.name: async reset with mismatched edge polarity
features.description: Code should include an always block with an async reset or set signal in the sensitivity list where the reset/set logic produces an active-high value but the sensitivity list uses a negative edge trigger, creating a polarity mismatch

features.name: signed expression in module port connection
features.description: Code should include a module instantiation where a signed expression is passed to an input port, which should be sign-extended to match the port width

features.name: hierarchical reference to localparam in instantiation port connection
features.description: Code should include a module instantiation where a hierarchical reference to a localparam of the instance (e.g., inst_name.param) is used as an argument in the port connection list of that same instance

features.name: localparam inside submodule accessible via hierarchical path
features.description: Code should include a module that defines localparams intended to be accessible from the parent scope via hierarchical references such as instance_name.localparam_name

features.name: multiple instances with per-instance hierarchical localparam port connections
features.description: Code should include multiple instances of the same module where each instance uses its own hierarchical localparam reference (e.g., e1.foo, e2.bar) as a port connection input to that respective instance

features.name: memory array with concurrent read and write ports
features.description: Code should include a register-array memory with both a read access and a write access in the same or related always blocks, producing both $memrd and $memwr cells that have a data-flow dependency on one another

features.name: priority mux selection feeding memory read address
features.description: Code should include a chain of if-else or nested conditional expressions selecting a memory read address from multiple sources, producing a $procmux cell whose output drives a memory read port ($memrd), so that the read cell depends on the mux cell in topological order

features.name: flattened hierarchy with memory and priority mux
features.description: Code should include a top-level module that instantiates a submodule containing both memory read/write operations and if-else priority selection, so that after hierarchy flattening and proc/pmuxtree passes the resulting netlist has $memwr, $memrd, and $procmux cells whose topological ordering must reflect read-after-write dependencies

features.name: reg signal driven by always_at_star without initialization
features.description: Code should include a registered signal declared as reg without initial value, where the signal is assigned inside an always @* block with blocking assignments controlled by a case statement

features.name: fsm state as continuous assign with always_at_star driven register
features.description: Code should include a wire signal assigned via continuous assignment that is used in an always @* sensitivity list which drives a registered signal, creating a situation where the always block may not trigger at simulation time 0

features.name: fsm combinational output using blocking assignment to register
features.description: Code should include an FSM pattern where a register is driven by combinational logic via blocking assignments inside an always @* block, where the register is not initialized and the FSM state never transitions

features.name: 2d_unpacked_array_memory_read_to_local_reg
features.description: Code should include a 2D unpacked array memory with a read operation inside a clocked always block, where the read result is assigned to a local reg variable (non-output) rather than an output port

features.name: memory_read_with_wire_index_in_clock_domain
features.description: Code should include a memory array read where the index uses a wire (computed value) rather than a registered value, inside a clocked always block

metadata.snippet:
wire [9:0] x_next = x + 1;
always @(posedge clk) pixel <= memory[x_next][y_next];

features.name: parameter-dependent port width declarations
features.description: Code should include a module parameter used in port bit-width declarations within the port list

metadata.snippet:
module foo #(parameter int W) (input [W-1:0] x, output [W-1:0] y);
endmodule

features.name: defparam with string parameter on instance
features.description: Code should include a defparam statement that assigns a string value to a parameter on a module instance

metadata.snippet:
defparam FIRST .extended_lut = "off";
defparam FIRST .shared_arith = "off";

features.name: defparam with hexadecimal literal parameter
features.description: Code should include a defparam statement that assigns a hexadecimal literal value to a parameter on a module instance

metadata.snippet: defparam FIRST .lut_mask = 64'h1212121212121212;

features.name: blackbox module instantiation with named ports
features.description: Code should include a module instantiation with named port connections that represents a blackbox component

metadata.snippet:
fourteennm_lcell_comb FIRST (
  .dataa(in_0),
  .datab(in_1),
  .combout(FIRST_combout));

features.name: function for-loop with function input as loop bound
features.description: Code should include a function containing a for-loop where the loop bound is a function input parameter accessed via part-select

features.name: function call with mixed constant and non-constant arguments
features.description: Code should include a function call where one argument is a constant parameter and another is a wire signal

features.name: modport explicit port renaming with dot notation

features.name: nested generate with same-name wire shadowing
features.description: Code should include nested generate blocks where an inner generate declares a wire using the same name as a wire declared in an outer generate block

metadata.snippet: generate for(l0=0;l0<4;l0=l0+1) begin : g0 wire [31:0] data = data_in[63:32]; for(l1=0;l1<4;l1=l1+1) begin : g1 wire [7:0] data = data_in[63:56]; end end endgenerate

features.name: genvar declared after nested initial block in generate
features.description: Code should include a genvar declaration placed after nested initial/always blocks within a generate block, where the genvar is used in a for loop at the same scope level

metadata.snippet: generate begin : cond initial begin : init reg signed [31:0] x; x = 2 ** 2; out1 = x; end always @* begin : proc reg signed [31:0] x; x = 2 ** 1; out2 = x; end genvar x; for (x = 0; x < 3; x = x + 1) assign out3[x] = 1; end endgenerate

features.name: partially-qualified scope reference in generate
features.description: Code should include a partially-qualified generate scope reference like B.y that should resolve to parent.A.B.y given the local context

metadata.snippet: generate begin : A begin : B wire [1:0] y = 2'b00; end begin : C wire [1:0] z = 2'b01; end wire [1:0] x = B.y ^ 2'b11 ^ C.z; end endgenerate

features.name: signed division of sign-cast unsigned product
features.description: Code should include a signed division where the dividend is an unsigned multiplication result cast to signed type

features.name: peepopt muldiv signedness mismatch
features.description: Code should include a peephole optimization scenario where muldiv pattern encounters operands with different signedness interpretations

features.name: combinational loop with shift operation

features.name: combinational feedback loop on output port

assign Y = S ? A + B : {4{t[0]}};

features.name: case statement default branch using wire
features.description: Code should include a case statement with a default branch that assigns a wire containing a computed expression

features.name: two additions sharing common operand with bit slicing

features.description: Code should include a right shift operation where the shift amount is declared as signed, which when incorrectly lowered by techmap causes incorrect evaluation results

metadata.snippet: input signed [5:0] B; wire [3:0] Y = A >> B;

features.name: shiftx cell instantiation with B_WIDTH exceeding connected signal width

features.name: shift with zero-padded concatenated shift amount
features.description: Code should include a right-shift operation where the shift amount is formed by concatenating a narrow signal with zero bits, effectively scaling the shift amount by a power of two

features.name: part-select with concatenated offset beyond array bounds returning x
features.description: Code should include a vector indexed part-select where the index offset can reach out-of-bounds positions, expecting 'x results for out-of-range accesses consistent with $shiftx semantics

features.name: packed struct as module port type
features.description: Code should include a packed struct type definition used as a module port (input or output)

features.name: inout port driven by assign from another signal
features.description: Code should include a module with an inout port that is driven by an assign statement from another signal

features.name: continuous assignment to escaped hierarchical signal after flatten
features.description: Code should include continuous assignments to backslash-escaped hierarchical signal identifiers that become unnecessary after module flattening

features.name: combinatorial module output assignment
features.description: Code should include a module output signal that is assigned procedurally in an always block without edge-trigger sensitivity, creating a combinatorial (non-registered) output

features.name: combinational feedback loop with multiple wire drivers
features.description: Code should include a combinational feedback loop where two wires drive each other, with at least one wire having an initial value driver and another assignment driver

features.name: range bounds using $high and $low with addition
features.description: Code should include a packed array range specification using $high and $low functions with arithmetic expressions in the bounds

features.name: DSP multiply with only A operand registered

features.name: task implicit single-bit input port with unsized constant argument

features.name: bit concatenation of task formal port with implicit width

features.name: task call width coercion from actual to formal port
features.description: Code should include a task invocation where the actual argument is an unsized integer literal (implicitly 32-bit) passed to a narrower formal port, testing that the formal port is truncated to its declared width at the call site

features.name: signed indexed part-select with variable offset
features.description: Code should include an upward indexed part-select (+:) where the base index is cast with $signed(), using a multi-bit signal as the dynamic offset

features.name: conditional signed shift with negative index detection
features.description: Code should include a combinational always block that checks whether a signal is negative using $signed() comparison, then performs a left shift for negative indices and a right shift for non-negative indices

features.name: x-literal masking in bitwise or for undefined shift bits
features.description: Code should include a 4'bx literal ANDed with a shift-derived bitmask and then ORed into a result to model undefined bits introduced by out-of-range shifts

features.name: constant_value_in_sensitivity_list_posedge
features.description: Code should include an always block with posedge sensitivity list containing a constant expression (e.g., posedge 1'b0)

features.name: constant_async_reset_in_always_block
features.description: Code should include a sequential always block with asynchronous reset using a constant value

features.name: constant_set_clear_in_sr_latch
features.description: Code should include a set-clear latch construct where set and/or clear uses a constant in the sensitivity list

features.name: empty parameter assignment in module instantiation
features.description: Code should include a module instantiation with a named parameter assignment where the parameter value expression is empty (just parentheses with no expression)

metadata.snippet: Blink #(.INIT ()) blink_inst (.clock(clock), .led(led));

features.name: multi_bit_function_return_bit_select_assignment
features.description: Code should include a function with multi-bit return value where different bits of the function name are assigned separately within the function body

features.name: function_call_result_for_indexed_assignment
features.description: Code should include a function call in an always block where the result is stored in a variable and then used as an index for conditional signal assignment

features.name: parameterized module with large bit-width arithmetic ports
features.description: Code should include a module with parameters defining large bit-widths (e.g., 64-bit inputs, 128-bit output) and parameterized port declarations

features.name: combinational always block with integer for loop and conditional

features.name: always_ff with intermediate output ports
features.description: Code should include an always_ff block that drives an output port which is also read internally within the same module (intermediate output pattern)

metadata.snippet:
module top (
  input  logic clk,
  input  logic d,
  output logic q
);
  always_ff @(posedge clk)
    q <= d & q;  // q is output AND used as feedback input
endmodule

features.name: always_ff register chain with intermediate outputs
features.description: Code should include multiple always_ff registers where output ports serve as intermediate signals (both driven by flip-flops and fed back as combinational inputs to the same always_ff block)

metadata.snippet:
module top (
  input  logic clk, rst,
  input  logic [3:0] d,
  output logic [3:0] q0, q1
);
  always_ff @(posedge clk or posedge rst)
    if (rst) begin q0 <= 0; q1 <= 0; end
    else begin
      q0 <= d ^ q1;  // q1 is output used as feedback
      q1 <= q0 | d;  // q0 is output used as feedback
    end
endmodule

features.description: Code should include variable bit select operations using hex notation for bounds

features.name: dff with async clear and preset in single always block
features.description: Code should include a flip-flop with both async clear and async preset signals in the same always block sensitivity list, with priority-based reset logic

features.name: module port list with named port connections using dot syntax

features.name: module instantiation with named port connections
features.description: Code should include module instantiation with named port connections using dot notation

features.name: reduction of concatenated comparison result
features.description: Code should include a reduction operator applied to a concatenated comparison result

features.name: multiple instances of same module
features.description: Code should include multiple instances of the same module connected to shared inputs

features.name: parameter with undefined bit in binary literal
features.description: Code should include a module instantiation with a parameter that uses a binary literal containing an undefined bit (x), such as 2'b0x

features.name: procedural_delayed_assignment
features.description: Code should include procedural block with #delay assignment that schedules value change at a future time, not immediately

metadata.snippet: always @(posedge clk) #5 q = d;

features.name: timing_annotation_in_sequential_logic
features.description: Code should include sequential logic (flip-flop) with timing-sensitive behavior that relies on simulation time ordering

metadata.snippet: always @(posedge clk) #1 reg_q <= data_in;

features.name: incomplete_if_statement_implicit_latch
features.description: Code should include an if statement without else branch, which can cause synthesis to infer a latch instead of combinational logic, leading to simulation mismatch.

metadata.snippet: always @(*) begin if (sel) q = d; end

features.name: priority_multiplexer_x_handling
features.description: Code should include a priority multiplexer with overlapping select conditions, where synthesis tools may convert priority encoding to parallel mux trees via pmuxtree, changing X-propagation behavior.

metadata.snippet: always @* begin if (sel == 2'b00) out = a; else if (sel == 2'b01) out = b; else out = c; end

features.name: memory_inference_with_uninitialized_locations
features.description: Code should include memory array with partial initialization, where memory synthesis pass may initialize locations differently than simulation.

metadata.snippet: reg [7:0] mem [0:255]; initial mem[0] = 8'hff;

features.name: flattened_hierarchy_name_resolution
features.description: Code should include multiple module instances with wires that get affected by flatten -scopename, which can cause name resolution differences during simulation.

metadata.snippet: module inner; wire [7:0] data; assign data = 8'hxx; endmodule

features.name: uninitialized_register_with_optimization
features.description: Code should include a register that is not initialized and gets optimized by opt -nodffe, which may remove initialization behavior differently than simulation.

metadata.snippet: reg [31:0] accum; always @(posedge clk) accum <= accum + 1;

features.name: RTLIL case rule with assign items after switch items
features.description: Code should include an RTLIL case rule where assign items appear after switch items, violating the internal assumption that assigns precede switches in case rule vectors

metadata.snippet:
case @
  switch 1
    assign 1- 1
  endswitch
  assign 1 1
endcase

features.name: comparison_mixed_signedness_with_real_arith
features.description: Code should include comparison between unsigned operand and result of integer parameter multiplied by real literal, where the multiplication result is treated as signed in the comparison cell

metadata.snippet: module test(input [31:0] a, output q); parameter VALUE = 100; assign q = a > VALUE * 1.2; endmodule

features.name: unsigned_signed_comparison_constant_expr
features.description: Code should include comparison between unsigned input [N:0] and signed constant expression derived from parameter arithmetic with real literal

metadata.snippet: assign q = a > VALUE * 1.2; // a is input [31:0], VALUE is integer parameter

features.name: partial_async_reset_slice_inference
features.description: Code should include a register with partial slice reset on async reset and full assignment in else branch, enabling bit-granular FF inference where different slices use $dffe vs $adff

metadata.snippet: always @(posedge clk or posedge rst) begin if (rst) q[3:0] <= '0; else q <= d; end

features.name: mixed_ff_control_same_signal
features.description: Code should include a register where different bit slices have different control structures - some bits with enable-only and others with async reset - allowing $dffe and $adff to be inferred separately

metadata.snippet: reg [7:0] q; always @(posedge clk or posedge rst) begin if (rst) q[3:0] <= '0; else q <= d; end

features.name: ROM memory initialization with readmemh
features.description: Code should include ROM-style memory initialization using $readmemh system task, which may cause topological sorting challenges in opt_expr pass when memory cells have complex address/data interconnections.

metadata.snippet: reg [7:0] rom [0:255]; initial $readmemh("rom_init.txt", rom);

features.name: Dense combinational cell fanout
features.description: Code should include designs with dense combinational cell fanout patterns that cause topological sorting failures in opt_expr when cell dependencies cannot be resolved linearly.

metadata.snippet: wire [15:0] data; assign data = {16{sel}} & rom_out; assign result = data & addr[15:0];

features.name: gate_instantiation_multiple_array_instances
features.description: Code should include a gate instantiation with multiple named instance arrays having different range specifications on the same declaration line.

metadata.snippet: nand #2 x_nand[0:3](out1, in1, in2), y_nand[4:7](out1, in1, in2);

features.name: register_with_feedback_inverter
features.description: Code should include a register that updates on posedge clock with a combinational feedback path where the register input is an inverted copy of the register output through a single NOT gate, creating a toggle behavior on each clock edge.

metadata.snippet: always @(posedge clk) q <= ~q; assign data_out = q;

features.name: output_port_connected_to_register
features.description: Code should include an output port that is directly connected to a register output, where the register updates on clock edge and the port value should remain stable between simulation steps when clock is stable.

metadata.snippet: output reg data_out; always @(posedge clk) data_out <= q;

features.name: back_to_back_step_without_clock_toggle
features.description: Code should include consecutive simulation steps (.step() calls) without clock toggles between them, where an output port driven by a register should maintain a stable value but may incorrectly change due to evaluation order issues in the simulation engine.

metadata.snippet: dut.p_clock.set(true); dut.step(); auto a = dut.p_data_out.get<int>(); dut.step(); auto b = dut.p_data_out.get<int>();

features.name: declaration_initialized_var_read_modify_write
features.description: Code should include a variable initialized in its declaration that is later subject to read-modify-write assignment (e.g., i = i + 1'b0) in an initial block, creating conflicting drivers when the tool evaluates both the declaration initializer and procedural assignment without proper sequencing.

features.name: procedural_block_multiple_value_sources
features.description: Code should include a variable that receives values from both its declaration initializer and a procedural assignment in an initial block, which causes the tool to generate conflicting drivers when it fails to model the sequential evaluation order specified by SV LRM 6.8.

metadata.snippet:
integer i = 1'b1;
initial i = 1'b0;

features.name: declaration_init_read_with_self_reference
features.description: Code should include a variable declared with an initializer that is read in an expression for its own assignment within an initial block, which triggers the tool to incorrectly treat declaration initializer and procedural assignment as concurrent drivers.

metadata.snippet:
integer i = 1'b1;
initial i = i + 1'b1;

features.name: signed_attribute_constant
features.description: Code should include signed attribute constants using the 's prefix in RTLIL attribute definitions with base-based notation (e.g., 32's00000000000000000000000000000100). Unsigned attributes (e.g., 32'00000000000000000000000000000100) should be distinguishable from signed ones of equal numeric value.

features.name: unsigned_attribute_constant
features.description: Code should include unsigned attribute constants without 's prefix in RTLIL attribute definitions with base-based notation (e.g., 32'00000000000000000000000000000100). Unsigned attributes should be distinguishable from signed ones of equal numeric value.

features.name: short_signed_attribute_constant
features.description: Code should include signed attribute constants with short bit widths (e.g., 5's00100). Short-width signed constants should be preserved distinctly from unsigned equivalents during RTLIL serialization round-trips.

metadata.snippet: attribute \c 5's00100

features.name: short_unsigned_attribute_constant
features.description: Code should include unsigned attribute constants with short bit widths (e.g., 5'00100). Short-width unsigned constants should be preserved distinctly from signed equivalents during RTLIL serialization round-trips.

metadata.snippet: attribute \d 5'00100

features.name: generate_for_with_3D_packed_array_xor
features.description: Code should include a generate-for loop iterating over 2D array indices with XOR operations across packed 3D array dimensions. The bug occurs when Yosys infers wide MUX structures instead of simple LUT4 when this pattern is used with modulo indexing in the loop body.

metadata.snippet:
genvar row, col;
generate
for (row = 0; row < 5; row++) begin
  for (col = 0; col < 5; col++) begin
    assign o_state[row][col] = i_state[row][col] ^ sum_sheet[(col - 1) % 5];
  end
end
endgenerate

features.name: array_slice_concatenation_with_modulo
features.description: Code should include array slicing combined with modulo-based index computation and concatenation of the slices. This pattern triggers wide MUX inference in Yosys when the -nowidelut flag is not used, causing exponential growth in LUT usage on ECP5 FPGAs.

features.name: xor_chain_across_array_dimension
features.description: Code should include a chain of XOR operations folding across one array dimension to compute a reduction, then using that result in subsequent XOR operations. This reduction pattern combined with generate-for loops triggers inefficient wide MUX synthesis on ECP5.

metadata.snippet: assign sum_sheet[i] = i_state[0][i] ^ i_state[1][i] ^ i_state[2][i] ^ i_state[3][i] ^ i_state[4][i];

features.name: non_contiguous_port_bit_selects
features.description: Code should include non-contiguous bit-selects on port wires when constructing output signals, such as assigning to a port using {wire[2], wire[0]} to create a non-contiguous connection pattern

features.name: port_width_mismatch_wrapper
features.description: Code should include mismatched port widths between wrapper module outputs and actual cell outputs, where the wrapper selectively includes only used output bits based on cell connections

metadata.snippet: output [2:0] y; wire [2:0] y_ = a | b; assign y = {y_[2], y_[0]};

features.name: keep_attribute_preservation
features.description: Code should include the (*keep*) attribute on wires to preserve them through optimization passes when used with cell wrapping operations

metadata.snippet: wire [2:0] y3_ = a3 | (*keep*) b3;

features.name: parameterized_module_instantiation
features.description: Code should include module instantiation with parameter overrides using #(.PARAM_NAME(value)) syntax

metadata.snippet:
TEST_CELL #(.PATH("TEST")) test1(.clk(clk),.a(a),.b(1'b1),.c(c));
TEST_CELL #(.PATH("DEFAULT")) test2(.clk(clk),.a(a),.b(1'bx),.c(d));

features.name: module_instantiation_with_literal_connection
features.description: Code should include module instantiation with literal values (1'b0, 1'b1, 1'bx, etc.) as port connections instead of signals

metadata.snippet: TEST_CELL #(.PATH("TEST")) test1(.clk(clk),.a(a),.b(1'b1),.c(c));

features.name: module_instantiation_with_x_value_connection
features.description: Code should include module instantiation with x value (1'bx) as port connection to a wire input

metadata.snippet: TEST_CELL #(.PATH("DEFAULT")) test2(.clk(clk),.a(a),.b(1'bx),.c(d));

features.name: blackbox_primitive_implicit_wire_port
features.description: Code should include instantiation of a blackbox technology-specific primitive (e.g., SB_PLL40_CORE for ice40) where an output port is connected to an implicitly-declared wire that is not declared in the module scope, relying on the tool to implicitly declare it.

metadata.snippet:
SB_PLL40_CORE #(
    .FEEDBACK_PATH("SIMPLE"),
    .PLLOUT_SELECT("GENCLK"),
    .DIVR(4'b0000),
    .DIVF(7'b0111111),
    .DIVQ(3'b100),
    .FILTER_RANGE(3'b001)
    ) uut (
    .LOCK(lock),
    .RESETB(1'b1),
    .BYPASS(1'b0),
    .REFERENCECLK(clkIn),
    .PLLOUTCORE(clkOut)
    );

features.name: technology_primitive_string_parameters
features.description: Code should include instantiation of a technology-specific blackbox primitive that accepts string-valued parameters (e.g., FEEDBACK_PATH, PLLOUT_SELECT) alongside binary numeric literals in the same parameter list.

metadata.snippet:
SB_PLL40_CORE #(
    .FEEDBACK_PATH("SIMPLE"),
    .PLLOUT_SELECT("GENCLK"),
    .DIVR(4'b0000),
    .DIVF(7'b0111111),
    .DIVQ(3'b100),
    .FILTER_RANGE(3'b001)
    ) uut ();

features.name: constant_literal_port_connections
features.description: Code should include a module instantiation where scalar constant values (1'b0, 1'b1) are directly connected to input ports via named port connection syntax, without intermediate wire declarations.

metadata.snippet:
.RESETB(1'b1),
    .BYPASS(1'b0),

features.name: primitive_instantiation_with_inverted_input
features.description: Code should include instantiation of xilinx sequential primitives (FDRE) with inverted signals at port connections, where the inversion is applied to a non-register input

features.name: LUT_INIT_bit_width_mismatch
features.description: Code should include a LUT primitive instantiation (LUT1-LUT8) where the INIT parameter has fewer bits than required. LUTn with n inputs requires INIT to have exactly 2^n bits. Using a smaller bit-width literal causes out-of-range vector access during optimization.

metadata.snippet: LUT1 #(.INIT(1'b1)) lut (.I0(in), .O(out));

features.name: undersized_binary_init_for_LUT
features.description: Code should include a binary literal with insufficient bit width used as the INIT parameter for a LUT primitive. A 1-bit literal like 1'b1 is invalid for LUT1 which requires a 2-bit INIT value (2^1=2). The synthesizer crashes with std::out_of_range.

metadata.snippet: LUT1 #(.INIT(1'b1)) lut_inst (.I0(1'b0), .O(led));

features.name: scopeinfo_cell_generation
features.description: Code should include hierarchy traversal with flatten -scopename to generate $scopeinfo cells for scope tracking in flattened designs

metadata.snippet:
hierarchy -top top
flatten -scopename

features.name: write_json_noscopeinfo_option
features.description: Code should include write_json -noscopeinfo option to exclude $scopeinfo cells from JSON output

metadata.snippet: write_json -noscopeinfo output.json

features.name: json_command_noscopeinfo_option
features.description: Code should include json -noscopeinfo option (alias for write_json) to exclude $scopeinfo cells from JSON output

metadata.snippet: json -noscopeinfo -o output.json

features.name: multi_module_scope_tracking
features.description: Code should include multiple module instances with flatten -scopename to generate $scopeinfo cells tracking scope hierarchy across module boundaries

features.name: large_nested_ternary_expression
features.description: Code should include a deeply nested ternary expression forming a large mux chain with many operands that causes deep recursion in synthesis tools.

features.name: wide_logical_and_expression
features.description: Code should include a wide logical AND expression with many operands that triggers deep recursion during AST simplification in synthesis tools.

features.name: implicit_port_connection
features.description: Code should include a module instantiation where a port is implicitly connected using only the port name without an explicit signal connection, e.g., .a instead of .a(signal).

metadata.snippet: foo foo (.a, .b, .c);

features.name: wildcard_port_connection
features.description: Code should include a module instantiation using the .* wildcard connection syntax to automatically connect all ports with matching names in the module.

metadata.snippet: foo foo (.*);

features.name: FSM next-state partial bit-select assignment
features.description: Code should include a next_state signal driven by indexed bit-select assignments within an always_comb block, where individual bits are set conditionally (e.g., next_state[S1] = 1'b1). This writing style may not be recognized by FSM detection tools.

metadata.snippet: always_comb begin next_state = 'b0; case (1'b1) current_state[S0]: begin next_state[S1] = 1'b1; end current_state[S1]: begin next_state[S2] = 1'b1; end endcase end

features.name: case(1'b1) state selection construct
features.description: Code should include a case(1'b1) construct used to decode one-hot state signals for FSM state transition logic. This is a unique pattern where the case expression is a constant 1'b1 and individual state bits are used as case item selectors.

metadata.snippet: case (1'b1) current_state[S0]: begin next_state[S1] = 1'b1; end current_state[S1]: begin next_state[S2] = 1'b1; end endcase

features.name: localparam state encoding with indexed access
features.description: Code should include localparam state definitions used as indices for bit-select access (e.g., localparam logic [1:0] S0 = 2'd0; current_state[S0]). This pattern combines localparam constants with indexed array access for state selection.

metadata.snippet: localparam logic [1:0] S0 = 2'd0; localparam logic [1:0] S1 = 2'd1; logic [3:0] current_state; current_state[S0]

features.name: multiplication_with_crosslinked_operands
features.description: Code should include a multiplication where one operand signal is derived from the other operand signal through shift/register operations, creating a cross-linked dependency that exceeds the DSP cell's single-user extraction constraint

metadata.snippet: reg1 <= {reg1[N-2:0], user_1}; reg2 <= {reg1[N-2:0], reg1[N-1]}; assign out = reg1 * reg2;

features.name: shift_register_via_concatenation_part_select
features.description: Code should include a shift register implemented using concatenation with part-select indexing {signal[N-2:0], signal[N-1]}, which when combined with multiplication creates DSP mapping issues on ice40

features.name: synth_ice40_dsp_size_sensitive_pattern
features.description: Code should include a multiplication pattern where register size N=40 triggers ice40 DSP mapping assertion failure while N=38 and N=42 work, indicating size-sensitive resource allocation

metadata.snippet: localparam N = 40; reg [N-1:0] reg1; reg [N-1:0] reg2; always @(posedge clk) reg1 <= {reg1[N-2:0], din};

features.name: async_reset_non_const_value
features.description: Code should include an always_ff block with asynchronous reset where the reset assignment uses a non-constant value (wire/variable input) rather than a literal constant.

metadata.snippet: always_ff @ (posedge clk or negedge rstn) if (!rstn) value <= rstval;

features.name: active_low_async_reset
features.description: Code should include an active-low asynchronous reset with negated condition in always_ff, using negedge in sensitivity list and !reset condition check.

metadata.snippet: always_ff @ (posedge clk or negedge rstn) if (!rstn) value <= rstval;

features.name: dff_with_arst_and_data_output
features.description: Code should include a D flip-flop with asynchronous reset where the reset value signal is a wire fed from another process or input, creating an $aldff cell.

metadata.snippet: wire value_next = ~value; always_ff @ (posedge clk or negedge rstn) if (!rstn) value <= rstval; else value <= value_next;

features.name: backslash-newline line continuation in attribute string literal
features.description: Code should include string literals with backslash-newline line continuation sequences inside attribute values

metadata.snippet:
(* attr="longlonglonglong\
longlonglongString" *)

features.name: constant_plus_toggle_reg_arithmetic
features.description: Code should include a wire formed by adding a constant to a concatenation of zero-padding and a toggling register, where the sum can never equal a specific comparison value.

features.name: unreachable_conditional_clock_assignment
features.description: Code should include a clocked always block where a register assignment is conditioned on a comparison that can never be true due to constant arithmetic on a toggling signal.

metadata.snippet: always @(posedge clk) if (b == 3'd0) c <= 1'b1;

features.name: toggle_register_concat_zero_extend
features.description: Code should include a register that toggles each clock cycle, zero-extended and used in an addition where the result is compared to a constant that can never match.

metadata.snippet: reg a = 1'b0; always @(posedge clk) a <= !a; wire [2:0] b = 3'd3 + { 2'b0, a };

features.name: constant_nonzero_minus_toggle_reg_comparison
features.description: Code should include a comparison between a constant N and an expression of the form (N + zero_extended_toggle), which can never equal zero.

features.name: sv_logic_keyword_in_non_sv_mode
features.description: Code should include 'logic' keyword that triggers parser failure when processed in Verilog 2005 mode instead of SystemVerilog mode

metadata.snippet: input  logic a

features.name: sv_logic_port_declaration
features.description: Code should include SystemVerilog 'logic' keyword used in port declarations within module header

features.name: assert_in_always_ff_with_async_reset
features.description: Code should include an always_ff block with dual-edge trigger (posedge clk or negedge async_reset) containing an assert statement that checks a condition when reset is active.

metadata.snippet: always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin if (rst_n) assert(1'b1); end

features.name: labeled_assert_statement
features.description: Code should include labeled assert statements with a label before the assert keyword

metadata.snippet: always @* begin label: assert(1); end

features.name: labeled_assume_statement
features.description: Code should include labeled assume statements with a label before the assume keyword

metadata.snippet: always @* begin label: assume(1); end

features.name: labeled_cover_statement
features.description: Code should include labeled cover statements with a label before the cover keyword

metadata.snippet: always @* begin label: cover(1); end

features.name: large_memory_with_combinational_for_loop_write
features.description: Code should include a large memory array (1024+ elements) with indexed writes inside a for-loop within a combinational always block, which triggers exponential memory growth in mem2reg_as_needed_pass2.

features.name: indexed_memory_write_in_for_loop
features.description: Code should include an indexed memory write operation using a loop variable inside a for-loop within an always @(*) block, causing excessive memory allocation during mem2reg conversion.

features.name: memory_with_integer_index_in_combinational_block
features.description: Code should include a memory array accessed with an integer variable index inside a combinational always block, which causes the mem2reg pass to create excessive intermediate structures.

features.name: continuous_assign_drives_unconnected_rhs
features.description: Code should include a continuous assignment where the right-hand side is an unconnected wire that is never driven elsewhere. This triggers a direction-reversal bug where the left-hand side incorrectly drives the right-hand side instead of the expected behavior where the right-hand side drives the left-hand side.

metadata.snippet: assign second_delay = first_delay;

features.name: output_wire_rhs_only
features.description: Code should include an output wire port that appears only on the right-hand side of a continuous assignment and is never driven anywhere else. Such wires should float but can trigger incorrect drive direction behavior in synthesis tools.

metadata.snippet: output wire first_delay

features.name: missing_doubly_driven_diagnostic
features.description: Code should include a signal that is driven by multiple sources (e.g., a DFF output and a continuous assignment) without the tool issuing a warning or error. This missing diagnostic allows direction-reversal bugs to go unnoticed.

metadata.snippet: assign second_delay = first_delay; // second_delay also driven by dff2

features.name: right_shift_by_max_int
features.description: Code should include right shift operation where shift amount is computed from bitwise NOT of a literal value (~31'b0 yields INT_MAX), which can cause integer overflow in synthesis tools if not capped to operand width.

features.name: shift_result_zero_when_shift_ge_width
features.description: Code should include shift operation where shift amount is equal to or exceeds the data width, which semantically should produce zero result regardless of operand value.

metadata.snippet: input signed [30:0] b; output [11:0] y = b >> ~31'b0;

features.name: conditional_indexed_memory_write
features.description: Code should include a conditional procedural assignment that writes to a memory location indexed by a computed address, where the address computation involves arithmetic or shifting operations on write enable signals

metadata.snippet: always @(posedge clk) begin if (wen[i]) big[wa[i]] <= wd[wa[i]]; end

features.name: multiported_memory_with_generate_reads
features.description: Code should include a large memory array with multiple read ports created via generate loop, where each read port uses an independently indexed address

metadata.snippet: logic [63:0][32:0] big; generate for (genvar i = 0; i < 9; i++) assign rd[i] = big[ra[i]]; endgenerate

features.name: diverging_converging_mux_chains
features.description: Code should include a design pattern where multiple conditional branches (from multiple indexed memory writes) converge on a shared data structure, creating many multiplexer cells with overlapping signal relationships

metadata.snippet: for (int i = 0; i < 9; i++) if (wen[i]) big[wa[i]] <= wd[wa[i]];

features.name: procedural_clock_control_with_reset
features.description: Code should include an always block with clocked logic and a conditional reset that initializes a large array, where subsequent operations within the block are control-dependent on the reset signal

metadata.snippet: always @(posedge clk) begin if (rst_clk) big <= '0; else begin for (int i = 0; i < 9; i++) if (wen[i]) big[wa[i]] <= wd[wa[i]]; end end

features.name: initial_block_with_write_task
features.description: Code should include an initial block containing $write system task calls. The $write task is a non-synthesizable integrated task that writes formatted strings during simulation. When present in a netlist for PnR tools like Gatemate, this causes incompatibility as PnR tools cannot process such initial blocks. The feature demonstrates the need to eliminate or avoid $write tasks in synthesizable output netlists.

metadata.snippet: initial begin if (1'h1) begin $write("Preloading $paramod\\serving_ram from %s\n", $unsigned(88'h636f72655f34362e686578)); end end

features.name: unsigned_cast_in_write_argument
features.description: Code should include a $unsigned() function call used within a $write task argument. This cast operation converts a value to unsigned type for display formatting. Such type conversions are common in display tasks but represent non-synthesizable constructs that should be eliminated before PnR.

features.name: conditional_write_initial_block
features.description: Code should include an if statement inside an initial block controlling a $write task. The condition is a constant expression (1'h1), which means the write will always execute. Such conditionally-controlled display tasks are non-synthesizable and should be removed for PnR compatibility.

metadata.snippet: if (1'h1) begin $write("Preloading $paramod\\serving_ram from %s\n", $unsigned(88'h636f72655f34362e686578)); end

features.name: hex_encoded_string_literal_in_write
features.description: Code should include a hex-encoded string literal inside a $write task. The string contains encoded data (e.g., 'core_46.hex') represented as hex values. These display-formatted strings with encoded parameter names are non-synthesizable constructs used for debugging output and should not appear in PnR-compatible netlists.

metadata.snippet: "Preloading $paramod$76ca5ad6cddf78bc518e7753a546bede55c5cafa\\serving_ram from %s\n"

features.name: latch_with_async_clear
features.description: Code should include a latch with asynchronous clear behavior where the data input flows through when the enable is active, and the latch holds state when enable is inactive. The clear asynchronously sets output to reset state regardless of enable. This models the standard latch behavior used in Liberty cell models where clear and preset signals override the data input.

metadata.snippet: always_latch begin if (async_clear) q = 1'b0; else if (!enable_n) q = data_in; end

features.name: latch_data_flow_when_enabled
features.description: Code should include a transparent latch where the output directly follows the data input while the enable signal is active. The data path must properly flow from input to output without being clobbered by enable logic. This captures the core bug where enable signals were incorrectly overriding data inputs in Liberty-described latches.

metadata.snippet: always_latch begin if (!gate_n) q = d; end

features.name: part_select_on_wire
features.description: Code should include a part-select operation on a wire using constant expression as range bounds, e.g., wire0[(2'h2):(1'h0)]

metadata.snippet:
wire [(2'h2):(1'h0)] wire4;
assign wire4 = (wire0[(2'h2):(1'h0)] ^~ $unsigned(wire1));

features.name: concatenation_with_unsized_literal
features.description: Code should include concatenation with unsized single-bit literal, e.g., {wire4, reg7, reg6, reg5, (1'h0)}

metadata.snippet: assign y = {wire4, reg7, reg6, reg5, (1'h0)};

features.name: width_mismatch_in_cell_output
features.description: Code should include signals of different widths driving the same expression output, creating width mismatch that triggers splitcells edge case

features.name: reduction_or_with_reg
features.description: Code should include reduction OR operator (~|) applied to a register in a ternary expression

features.name: signed_cast_of_wider_signal
features.description: Code should include $signed cast applied to a signal that is wider than its destination in an assignment

features.name: ice40_bram_inference_boundary_size
features.description: Code should include a SystemVerilog memory array declaration with size [0:(512*N)-1] where N is a value in {23, 27, 31}, which triggers an off-by-one boundary bug in Yosys synth_ice40 BRAM inference causing incorrect extra BRAM allocation.

metadata.snippet: reg [7:0] mem [0:(512*NUM_BRAMS)-1]; // NUM_BRAMS in {23, 27, 31} triggers synth_ice40 bug

features.name: ice40_bram_size_formula_8n_minus_1
features.description: Code should include a BRAM count parameter that equals 8*n-1 (e.g., 23, 27, 31), which when used in memory size calculation (512 * (8*n-1)) triggers synth_ice40 incorrect BRAM inference.

metadata.snippet:
localparam NUM_BRAMS = 27; // 8*n-1 pattern triggers bug
reg [7:0] mem [0:(512*NUM_BRAMS)-1];

features.name: procedural_memory_write
features.description: Code should include a procedural memory write operation inside a clocked always block, where a memory element is assigned synchronously using non-blocking assignment.

metadata.snippet: always @(posedge clk) begin if (en) ram[addr] <= data; end

features.description: Code should include a procedural memory read operation inside a clocked always block, where a memory element is read synchronously.

metadata.snippet: always @(posedge clk) begin data_out <= ram[addr]; end

features.name: async_reset_in_clock_block
features.description: Code should include an asynchronous reset condition inside a clocked always block, where reset is checked alongside the clock edge.

metadata.snippet: always @(posedge clk or posedge reset) begin if (reset) q <= 0; else q <= d; end

features.name: hilomap_wrap_constant
features.description: Code should include constant arithmetic expressions that may be wrapped by hilomap -wrap command for coarse-grain device mapping

metadata.snippet: assign out = in + 16

features.name: initial_block_with_display
features.description: Code should include an initial block containing $display system task for simulation-time messaging

metadata.snippet: initial if(|memfile) begin $display("Preloading %m from %s", memfile); $readmemh(memfile, mem); end

features.name: initial_block_with_readmemh
features.description: Code should include an initial block with $readmemh for loading memory contents from file at simulation start

metadata.snippet: initial if(|memfile) begin $display("Preloading %m from %s", memfile); $readmemh(memfile, mem); end

features.name: conditional_initial_block_with_file_variable
features.description: Code should include a conditional initial block using a file path variable to conditionally execute simulation-only operations

metadata.snippet: initial if(|memfile) begin $display("Preloading %m from %s", memfile); $readmemh(memfile, mem); end

features.name: module_instance_localparam_override
features.description: Code should include a module instance with parameter override syntax targeting a localparam. The child module declares a localparam that is then attempted to be overridden via instance parameter override syntax (dot notation) from a parent module.

features.name: blif_gate_initial_state_suffix
features.description: Code should include sequential gate declarations with numeric initial state suffix in BLIF format. The suffix (0=reset, 1=set, 2=undefined/high-z) appears after the gate type name before port definitions. This non-standard extension encodes initialization state for DFFs and latches.

metadata.snippet: .gate DFF 0 C=clk D=$abc$372906$auto$rtlil.cc:2985:MuxGate$372067 Q=\5.internal_registers[533]

features.name: DSP output partial bit usage with nusers check
features.description: Code should include a DSP multiplier cell (SB_MAC16) where only lower bits of the 16-bit output are connected to downstream arithmetic, leaving upper bits unused. The design uses nusers() to identify unused wire bits during optimization.

metadata.snippet: cell \SB_MAC16 . parameter \TOPOUTPUT_SELECT 2'11 connect \O { $test $delete_wire$514 14'x ... } cell $add ... connect \A $delete_wire$514 [1:0]

features.name: Reverse iteration for unused bit detection
features.description: Code should include a loop that iterates from the end of a signal backward to detect trailing unused bits, using nusers() to identify wire bits with no downstream consumers. Forward iteration from bit 0 fails to correctly identify unused bits in DSP output patterns.

metadata.snippet: for (i = GetSize(O) - 1; i > 0 && nusers(O[i]) <= 1; i--);

features.name: Empty wire insertion for bit offset alignment
features.description: Code should include logic that inserts empty wires (width > 0, no users) to maintain bit offset alignment when upper bits of a DSP output are unused but required for position mapping in downstream operations.

metadata.snippet: if (nusers(O[j]) == 0) wire_width++; else { if (wire_width) sigH.append(module->addWire(NEW_ID, wire_width)); wire_width = 0; }

features.name: constant_array_256_entry_lookup
features.description: Code should include a large constant array (256 entries) initialized with explicit values, indexed by a computed value to produce output.

features.name: array_indexed_via_function_call
features.description: Code should include an array element accessed through a function call that computes the index, such as using conversion function on an unsigned input to index into a constant array.

features.name: async_read_memory_array
features.description: Code should include a memory array with asynchronous read where the read operation occurs outside of a clocked always block, causing the memory to be inferred as $mem_v2

metadata.snippet: reg [3:0] m[60:0]; always @(posedge clk) begin y <= m[a]; m[a] <= w; end

features.name: combinational_multiplication
features.description: Code should include a combinational multiplication assignment that gets lowered to $macc_v2 after alumacc pass

metadata.snippet: assign y = a * b;

features.name: keep_hierarchy_min_cost_command
features.description: Code should include the keep_hierarchy command with a -min_cost threshold for controlling hierarchy preservation based on cell cost estimation

metadata.snippet: keep_hierarchy -min_cost 20

features.name: memory_array_with_wide_address
features.description: Code should include a memory array accessed via a wide address input where the address width exceeds the memory depth, triggering $mem_v2 cost estimation

metadata.snippet: reg [3:0] m[60:0]; always @(posedge clk) begin y <= m[a]; m[a] <= w; end

features.name: Feedback loop through module instantiation
features.description: Code should include a feedback path that crosses module boundaries: a wire driven by a register output is passed to an instance input port, and the same wire is also driven by the instance's output port, creating a loop through module hierarchy.

features.name: Expression-driven instance port connection
features.description: Code should include an instance input port connected to a compound expression rather than a simple wire or port reference, where the expression references a wire also driven by the instance's output.

metadata.snippet: .in_b((out_q & i_in))

features.name: Registered wire driving hierarchical feedback
features.description: Code should include a register whose output wire forms part of a feedback path that crosses module boundaries, where the same wire is both the register's output and connected to an instance output port.

reg out_q;
always @(posedge clk) begin if (rst) out_q <= 0; else out_q <= in_b; end

features.name: defparam localparam override attempt
features.description: Code should include a defparam statement that targets a localparam in a child module instance, which is illegal and should produce a clear error message indicating the localparam cannot be overridden.

features.name: module hierarchy with defparam to child instance
features.description: Code should include a parent module that uses defparam to override a parameter in a child module instance, where the targeted parameter is declared as localparam making the override illegal.

features.name: module with mixed parameter and localparam
features.description: Code should include a module parameter section that mixes regular parameters with localparam declarations, demonstrating that localparam should not be overridable via defparam.

features.name: primitive_instantiation_with_unconnected_port
features.description: Code should include instantiation of a flip-flop or latch primitive with an unconnected or partially connected port that was expected by the tool to be present during optimization passes.

metadata.snippet: FDSE fdse_inst (.C(clk), .D(data), .CE(1'b1), .Q(q));

features.name: synchronous_set_ff_primitive
features.description: Code should include instantiation of a flip-flop with synchronous set capability using a technology-specific primitive where the set port is left unconnected.

metadata.snippet: FDSE fdse_inst (.C(clk), .D(data), .CE(1'b1), .Q(q));

features.name: synth_xilinx_with_partial_primitive_connection
features.description: Code should include a design using synth_xilinx command with flip-flop primitives that have optional ports left unconnected, triggering optimization passes like XILINX_DFFOPT to access unconnected port references.

metadata.snippet: synth_xilinx -flatten -nowidelut -arch xc7 -top top

features.name: shift_with_wider_unsigned_input_than_output
features.description: Code should include a shift operation where the shift amount input has a larger unsigned bit-width than the result output width, causing width mismatch during cell splitting.

metadata.snippet: assign y = a << b; // B_WIDTH > Y_WIDTH with B unsigned

features.name: ternary_with_x_value_condition
features.description: Code should include a ternary expression where the condition resolves to x (undefined), triggering simplified internal cells with mismatched port widths during processing.

metadata.snippet: assign y = cond ? (a << b) : 1'b0; // cond is unknown/x

features.name: 2D unpacked array memory with parameterized dimensions
features.description: Code should include a 2D unpacked array memory declaration where the packed dimension uses parameterized bit width and unpacked dimensions use parameterized array size, which may trigger incorrect BRAM mapping in certain synthesis tools.

metadata.snippet: reg [RGB_PIXEL_WIDTH-1:0] frame_buffer[RESOLUTION_X][RESOLUTION_Y]

features.name: 2D array access with independent x/y index variables

features.name: Multi-field concatenation for pixel/color data packing
features.description: Code should include concatenation of multiple fields with different bit widths (R/G/B pixel components) to form a packed pixel value, which when used with 2D array storage may cause BRAM data width mapping issues.

features.name: Bit slicing on unpacked array memory output

features.name: always_block_event_control_attribute

features.description: Code should include named endmodule syntax with colon label: `endmodule : module_name`

features.name: power_of_2_base_optimization_with_signed_exponent
features.description: Code should include power expression where base is a power of 2 (e.g., 128) and exponent is a signed expression that can produce negative values, triggering incorrect optimization in opt_expr

features.name: pow_cell_power_of_2_base_with_signal_exponent
features.description: Code should include $pow cell where A port connects to a power-of-2 constant (2, 128, 256, etc.) and B port is a signal or operation, subject to unsigned-only optimization constraint in opt_expr

features.name: unsigned_base_power_with_signed_arithmetic_exponent
features.description: Code should include power expression where unsigned base signal is raised to exponent derived from signed arithmetic that can produce negative values, exposing unsigned-only optimization constraint flaw

features.name: variable_width_shift_in_always_ff
features.description: Code should include a right shift operation (>>) where the shift amount is a multi-bit register variable rather than a constant. This creates variable-length shift behavior that can cause unpredictable synthesis results.

metadata.snippet: if (b >> e) begin d = e; end

features.name: self_canceling_arithmetic_condition
features.description: Code should include an arithmetic expression that subtracts a variable from itself (e.g., c - c), which always evaluates to zero. This creates a constant-false condition that can confuse synthesis optimizations.

metadata.snippet: if (c - c) begin e = 0; end

features.name: combinational_toggle_feedback
features.description: Code should include a register assignment that toggles its value (!e) inside a combinational always block. This creates an unstable feedback loop where the register changes every evaluation cycle, potentially causing synthesis tools to fail to converge.

metadata.snippet: e = !e;

features.name: dff_inverter_merge_oscillation
features.description: Code should include a latch inference pattern with feedback that causes synthesis tools to oscillate between inverted and non-inverted latch states. The pattern involves registers with toggle behavior driving latch control signals, leading to infinite loops in DFF optimization passes.

metadata.snippet: always @(*) begin if (b >> e) begin d = e; end e = !e; end

features.name: scl_cell_preassigned_placement
features.description: Code should include standard cell library cells with pre-assigned placement locations (pPlace != -1) that trigger assertion failures in ABC's hash-based cell registration.

features.name: multi_cell_lib_with_sequential_skip
features.description: Code should include standard cell libraries containing both combinational and sequential cells where sequential cells are skipped during processing but still trigger hash table assertion conflicts.

features.name: abc_hashcells_assertion_on_placement
features.description: Code should include constructs that cause ABC's Abc_SclHashCells to fail an assertion checking that pPlace equals -1, indicating cells were already placed before hash registration.

metadata.snippet:
// Trigger: library cell processed by ABC with non-default placement
assert(*pPlace == -1); // fails when cell has existing placement

features.name: undefined_mux_all_ports
features.description: Code should include a multiplexer where the output and at least two data inputs are all connected to undefined values (1'bx or 1'x), creating a degenerate MUX that causes treeification stalls in muxcover

features.name: undefined_select_with_disconnected_output
features.description: Code should include a multiplexer where the select input is valid but the output is disconnected (undefined), potentially causing stalls in optimization passes that propagate undefined values

features.name: multiplexer_x_operands
