features.name: multi_driver_register_mixed_sensitivity
features.description: Code should include a register driven by both a clocked always block and a combinational always block with different sensitivity lists.

features.name: nested_ternary_register_assignment
features.description: Code should include nested ternary operators in a register assignment within a clocked always block.

metadata.snippet: always @(posedge b or negedge c) begin if (c) l = (l | j) ? k ? h : i ? 0 : n : 0; end

features.name: async_load_ff_with_enable
features.description: Code should include a register with async load that gets enable signal added during DFF optimization, triggering infinite loop in synth_efinix.

features.name: defparam_in_generate_block
features.description: Code should include a defparam statement inside a generate block that overrides a parameter on a module instantiated within the same generate block. The defparam uses hierarchical path notation (instance_name.parameter_name) to reference the parameter.

features.name: generate_block_module_instantiation
features.description: Code should include a module instantiated inside a generate block using a local instance name, where the instantiation uses named port connections and the instance is referenced by a defparam in the same generate block scope.

features.name: ternary_with_self_referential_shift
features.description: Code should include a ternary expression where the same variable is used both as the condition and as the shift amount for a bitwise shift operation, creating a combinational loop that causes infinite recursion in XAIGER backend.

features.name: variable_shift_in_ternary_condition
features.description: Code should include a ternary where a register variable controls both the condition and the shift amount, with the shift result used in a case statement that assigns back to the register.

features.name: non_zero_indexed_register_in_feedback
features.description: Code should include a reg variable with non-zero based index (e.g., [2:7]) used in a feedback pattern within an always @(*) block, where the register reads and writes occur in the same always block.

reg [2:7] e;

always @(*) begin
    case (e ? b << e : 0)
        'd67: begin
            e = c;
        end
    endcase
end

features.name: wide_input_port_with_large_bit_range
features.description: Code should include a wide input port declaration with a large non-zero-based bit range such as [83:10] or similar. This creates many intermediate wires during synthesis that stress the AUTONAME pass scoring mechanism.

metadata.snippet: input [83:10] c

features.name: division_chain_in_combinational_block
features.description: Code should include chained division operations in a combinational always block, such as b / e / c. These operations generate multiple intermediate division results that produce high wire rename scores.

metadata.snippet: g = b ? b / e / c : 0

features.name: division_result_in_if_condition
features.description: Code should include a division result used as an if condition, such as if (d / g). This pattern triggers complex wire naming during the AUTONAME pass.

features.name: mixed_blocking_and_nonblocking_in_same_always
features.description: Code should include both blocking (=) and non-blocking (<=) assignments to the same variable (g) within a single combinational always block. This creates conflicting assignments that generate many intermediate wires.

features.name: reduction_xor_feedback
features.description: Code should include a unary reduction XOR operator where the result is assigned back to the same variable used as input, creating a feedback loop through a reduction operation.

metadata.snippet: c = ^c;

features.name: case_default_only
features.description: Code should include a case statement with only a default case item and no explicit case labels, which creates implicit case matching behavior.

metadata.snippet:
case (0)
    default: begin
        b = c;
    end
endcase

features.name: vector_range_high_low
features.description: Code should include a vector declaration with a non-standard range where the high index is greater than the low index, using reversed or non-contiguous indexing.

metadata.snippet: reg [2:1] c;

features.name: multiplication_in_ternary_condition
features.description: Code should include a multiplication operation used as the condition of a ternary operator, where the operands include both a wire and a reg.

metadata.snippet: f = d * h ? 0 : g;

features.name: combinational_always_with_case_and_register_assignment
features.description: Code should include a combinational always block with a case statement that assigns to registers, creating feedback loops between the case condition and register updates.

metadata.snippet: always @(*) begin e = f; case (f) 'd1: begin f = d * h ? 0 : g; h = f + i; end endcase end

metadata.snippet: wire [31:0] d; reg [31:0] h; always @(*) f = d * h;

features.name: register_cross_dependency_in_case

features.name: vectored_multi_bit_reg_declaration

case (g << j >> h)
    32'd3: begin
        case (b << j)
            'd1: begin
                h = 0;
                j = f ? (^k) : 0;
            end
        endcase
    end
endcase

features.name: bit_shift_on_vectored_reg
features.description: Code should include bit shift operations (<< or >>) performed on multi-bit reg vectors.

case (g << j >> h)
    32'd3: begin
        case (b << j)
            'd1: begin end
        endcase
    end
endcase

features.name: ternary_with_reduction_in_branch
features.description: Code should include a ternary operator where one branch contains a reduction operator expression.

features.name: multiple_always_blocks_mixed_sensitivity
features.description: Code should include multiple always blocks with different sensitivity types (combinational @(*) and clocked @(signal)).

metadata.snippet:
always @(*) begin
    c = h;
    case (b)
        'd1: h = i;
    endcase
end

always @(f) begin
    case (g << j >> h)
        32'd3: begin end
    endcase
end

features.name: logic_or_mismatched_widths
features.description: Code should include a logic OR operation where operands have mismatched bit widths (e.g., 1-bit and multi-bit).

metadata.snippet: h = e || b; // b is [1:0], e is 1-bit

features.name: synth_efinix_with_complex_case_and_shift
features.description: Code should include the pattern of nested case with bit shifts on vectored regs that triggers ABC assertion during synth_efinix.

metadata.snippet:
module a(input f, input [1:0] b, output reg c);
    reg [1:0] g, h, i, j;
    wire k, e;
    always @(*) begin
        c = h;
        case (b)
            'd1: h = i;
        endcase
    end
    always @(f) begin
        case (g << j >> h)
            32'd3: begin
                case (b << j)
                    'd1: begin h = 0; j = f ? (^k) : 0; end
                    'd2: begin i = j; end
                endcase
            end
            default: h = e || b;
        endcase
    end
endmodule

features.name: mux_all_inputs_x_undefined
features.description: Code should include a multiplexer (mux) operation where all data inputs are connected to undefined ('x) values, which can occur when a wider selection signal selects from a narrower data source.

features.name: mux_wide_sel_narrow_data
features.description: Code should include a multiplexer where the selection signal bit-width is larger than the number of data inputs available, causing unused selection patterns to reference undefined values.

metadata.snippet: wire [1:0] sel; wire a, b; assign o = sel[1:0] == 2'b0 ? a : (sel[1:0] == 2'b1 ? b : 1'bx); // sel can be 2'b10 or 2'b11 with no corresponding data input

features.name: nested_mux_chain_with_x
features.description: Code should include a chain of nested multiplexer operations where inner muxes resolve to undefined values ('x) when selection logic references out-of-range indices.

features.name: bram_initialization_with_readmemb
features.description: Code should include a memory array (BRAM) initialized via $readmemb system task in an initial block

features.name: readmemb_with_explicit_address_range
features.description: Code should include $readmemb system task with explicit start and end address parameters for partial memory initialization

metadata.snippet: $readmemb("init.txt", memory, 0, n_elements-1);

features.name: bram_with_sequential_write_after_initialization
features.description: Code should include a BRAM memory initialized via readmemb in an initial block, followed by a clocked write operation to a different address

features.name: empty_begin_end_block
features.description: Code should include a begin...end block with no statements or logic inside. This tests tools that must handle empty control flow blocks without assertion failures.

metadata.snippet:
begin
end

features.name: module_with_empty_begin_end
features.description: Code should include a module with input/output ports containing only empty begin...end blocks with no actual driving logic. This tests tools that must handle modules where outputs are undriven.

metadata.snippet:
module top(input a, output b);
  begin
  end
endmodule

features.name: keep_attribute_on_memory_array
features.description: Code should include the (* keep *) attribute directly on a memory array declaration to prevent optimization passes from removing or merging the memory.

metadata.snippet: (* keep *) logic [WIDTH-1:0] mem[SIZE-1:0];

features.name: keep_attribute_on_module_instance
features.description: Code should include the (* keep=1 *) attribute on a module instance to prevent optimization passes from eliminating or merging the instantiated module.

metadata.snippet: (* keep=1 *) comb_mem_d1 #(.WIDTH(32), .SIZE(16)) data1 (...);

features.name: memory_nomap_before_opt
features.description: Code should include memory -nomap followed by opt -full to preserve memory structures while allowing other optimizations, testing whether keep attributes are respected.

metadata.snippet:
memory -nomap
opt -full

features.name: fsm_state_register_no_initialization
features.description: Code should include an FSM state register declared without an initial value assignment

metadata.snippet: logic [3:0] current_state;

features.name: undefined_module_instantiation_in_equiv_check
features.description: Code should include module instantiations of undefined/blackbox modules that differ between gold and gate designs, causing equiv_opt to falsely claim equivalence when SAT models are unavailable for those cells.

features.name: wire_name_with_slash
features.description: Code should include wire names containing forward slash character (/), which requires proper escaping or quoting in Yosys selection commands

metadata.snippet: wire \aa/N1

features.name: selection_syntax_with_colon_prefix
features.description: Code should include selection commands using n:\ prefix syntax for named wires in Yosys ILANG format

metadata.snippet: select -set testsel n:\aa/N1

features.name: ilang_wire_declaration
features.description: Code should include ILANG format wire declarations using backslash-prefixed identifiers with wire keyword

metadata.snippet: wire \aa/N1

features.name: bwmux_internal_cell_instantiation
features.description: Code should include $bwmux cell instantiations with WIDTH parameter for bit-width multiplexing between two values based on select signal

metadata.snippet: \$bwmux #(.WIDTH(8)) mux (.A(A), .B(B), .S(S), .Y(Y));

features.name: 4-to-1 mux with 2-bit select evaluated with set-undef
features.description: Code should include a $_MUX4_ cell (4-to-1 mux with two select inputs S and T) being evaluated with the eval command using -set-undef flag, where the cell is incorrectly marked as evaluable causing infinite loops when select inputs are undefined

metadata.snippet:
cell $_MUX4_ \UUT
  connect \A \A
  connect \B \B
  connect \C \C
  connect \D \D
  connect \S \S
  connect \T \T
  connect \Y \Y
end

features.name: single_port_ram_with_separate_write_clock
features.description: Code should include a single-port RAM inference pattern with separate read address register and write address from shared bus, where read and write share the same address port based on mode

features.name: registered_read_enable_signal
features.description: Code should include a registered read enable signal that captures the read/write mode at clock edge before being used to select the registered read address

metadata.snippet:
reg reg_RW0_ren;
always @(posedge RW0_clk) reg_RW0_ren <= RW0_en && !RW0_wmode;

features.name: mode_based_read_write_on_shared_address
features.description: Code should include a memory access pattern where write occurs on positive clock edge when write mode is active, and read uses a registered address from the same address input when not in write mode

features.name: integer_literal_width_truncation
features.description: Code should include integer literals assigned to narrower bit-width values, which must be implicitly truncated to fit the target width.

metadata.snippet: logic [W-1:0] var = CONST;

features.name: async_or_clock_register_with_combinational_output
features.description: Code should include registers updated in an always block with OR'd asynchronous clock edges (posedge clock_0 or posedge clock_1), where the output is a combinational function of those registers

features.name: dual_clock_register_assignment
features.description: Code should include registers that receive different non-blocking assignment values based on which of two clock signals is active, with the same register receiving different values depending on clock selection

features.name: arithmetic cells with interlocked add/sub output-input chaining
features.description: Code should include a $add cell whose output wire feeds back as input to a $sub cell, creating interlocked arithmetic operations

metadata.snippet:
cell $add $add$753
  parameter \A_SIGNED 0 \A_WIDTH 2 \B_SIGNED 0 \B_WIDTH 2 \Y_WIDTH 2
  connect \A $sub_Y
  connect \Y $auto$bugpoint.cc:258:simplify_something$928
end
cell $sub $sub$752
  parameter \A_SIGNED 0 \A_WIDTH 2 \B_SIGNED 0 \B_WIDTH 2 \Y_WIDTH 2
  connect \A $auto$bugpoint.cc:258:simplify_something$923
  connect \Y $sub_Y
end

features.name: alumacc pass followed by write_verilog on arithmetic design
features.description: Code should include an RTLIL design with $add and $sub cells that is first processed by the alumacc pass then output via write_verilog backend

metadata.snippet: read_rtlil design.rtlil; alumacc; write_verilog -sv output.v

features.name: division_with_oversized_concat_divisor
features.description: Code should include division where the divisor is a concatenation of a single-bit constant and a wider signal, producing a value that exceeds the declared bit width of the divisor operand, causing ctlz() to return an anomalous value and triggering an assertion failure in udivmod.

features.name: unsigned_division_operand_width_mismatch
features.description: Code should include unsigned division where an operand's value exceeds its declared bit width, causing ctlz() to produce invalid results that lead to division algorithm failures in CXXRTL.

metadata.snippet: wire [6:0] divisor = {1'b1, wide_signal}; wire [6:0] result = dividend / divisor;

features.name: division_with_value_exceeding_bit_width
features.description: Code should include division where the actual value of an operand (after assignment or concatenation) exceeds what can be represented in its declared bit width, leading to runtime assertion failures in the division routine.

metadata.snippet: input wire in2; wire [6:0] divisor = {1'b1, in2}; assign out = 7'b11100 / divisor;

features.name: large_constant_right_shift_on_small_signed_input
features.description: Code should include a right shift operation on a small signed packed array by a constant that exceeds the input bit width

metadata.snippet:
input wire signed [21:18] in1;
assign out2 = in1 >> 32;

features.name: empty_param_value_named_port_style
features.description: Code should include an empty parameter value using named port style syntax where a parameter is specified with empty parentheses and no assigned value

metadata.snippet: bar #(.paramname()) barinstance ();

features.name: empty_parameter_list
features.description: Code should include an empty parameter list using #() syntax in module instantiation

metadata.snippet: bar #() barinstance (.portname());

features.name: signed_shift_with_sign_extension
features.description: Code should include signed right shift of a signed operand where the operand's width is narrower than the expression result width, triggering implicit sign-extension before the shift operation.

metadata.snippet: assign out0 = reg_7 >> in2; // reg_7 is signed [9:0], out0 is [12:0]

features.name: variable_shift_amount
features.description: Code should include right shift operation where the shift amount is a dynamic value from a wire or input rather than a compile-time constant.

metadata.snippet: reg_7 >> in2  // in2 is an input wire, not a constant

features.name: implicit_sign_extension_in_assignment
features.description: Code should include assignment from a signed operand to a wider result type where the operand must be sign-extended to match the expression width before the operation.

features.name: signed memory with bit-select on accessed element
features.description: Code should include a signed memory access followed by a bit-select on the result, where the bit-select index is out of range for the memory element width

metadata.snippet:
reg signed ra [1];
always @(posedge clk) rb[0] = ra[0] + ra[0][0];

features.name: addition with mixed memory read and bit-select
features.description: Code should include an addition operation where one operand is a memory read and the other is a bit-select of a memory read from the same memory

features.name: large_fanout_wire_with_long_alias_names
features.description: Code should include a single wire with fanout exceeding ~90000 bits combined with long alias names exceeding 2000 characters, which triggers integer overflow in Yosys autoname when wire score exceeds INT32_MAX/20000

metadata.snippet:
wire [89999:0] fanout_wire; // single wire fanning out to ~90000 destinations
// followed by many assign statements creating long names > 2000 chars

features.name: transparent memory read port with registered address
features.description: Code should include a memory array with a write port and a read port whose address is registered via an intermediate register before memory access, creating transparent read behavior

features.name: memory with rw_addr_collision attribute for Vivado
features.description: Code should include a memory array declaration with the rw_addr_collision attribute set to yes, which is needed to suppress Vivado warnings for transparent read ports

metadata.snippet: (* rw_addr_collision = "yes" *) reg [20:0] mem [127:0];

features.name: multi_clock_async_sensitivity_false_positive_reset_detection
features.description: Code should include always block with multiple posedge clocks where one clock signal is used in an if condition inside the block, triggering false async reset detection that incorrectly simplifies the condition to a constant.

metadata.snippet: always @(posedge clk_a or posedge clk_b) begin if (clk_b) x <= 1; else x <= 0; end

features.name: conditional_clock_branch_erasure_by_proc_pass
features.description: Code should include if statement checking a clock signal inside an always block sensitive to that same clock, where Yosys proc pass incorrectly replaces the branch condition with constant 1'h0, making the true branch unreachable.

metadata.snippet: always @(posedge c0 or posedge c1) begin if (c1) $display("c1 true"); else $display("c1 false"); end

features.name: partial_width_reg_assignment_with_clock_dependent_conditional
features.description: Code should include partial-width slice assignment to a register (e.g., reg[28:26] <= 2) inside an if-else branch that is conditional on a signal also used in the always block's sensitivity list, triggering false reset detection.

metadata.snippet: always @(posedge clock_0 or posedge clock_2) begin if (clock_2) begin end else begin if (in0) reg_10[28:26] <= 2; else reg_10[28:27] <= 1; end end

features.name: signed_reg_partial_slice_assignment
features.description: Code should include signed register with partial slice assignment (non-contiguous or narrower than full reg width) inside conditional branches of a clocked always block.

features.name: display_in_always_block_with_multi_clock_sensitivity
features.description: Code should include $display statement inside always block sensitive to multiple clocks where one clock's conditional branch is incorrectly optimized, causing display of wrong branch on clock edge.

metadata.snippet: always @(posedge c0 or posedge c1) begin $display("triggered"); if (c1) $display("c1 branch"); else $display("c0 branch"); end

features.name: modulus_with_ternary_divisor
features.description: Code should include a modulus operation where the divisor is a ternary conditional expression with vector slice operands.

metadata.snippet: assign r = 32'hA2F % { 1'b1 ? b : c};

features.name: ternary_with_vector_part_select
features.description: Code should include a ternary conditional expression where both branches are part-selects from the same vector.

features.name: hex_constant_modulo_expression
features.description: Code should include a large hex constant used in a modulo expression with a conditional divisor.

metadata.snippet: 32'ha2f % { cond ? a : b}

features.name: blackbox_module_with_attribute
features.description: Code should include a module marked with (* blackbox *) attribute to indicate it has no internal implementation

features.name: module_instantiation_with_named_ports
features.description: Code should include module instantiation using named port connections

features.name: hierarchy_top_module_selection
features.description: Code should include hierarchy command with -top flag to set the top module for synthesis

metadata.snippet: hierarchy -top sub_mod

features.name: cutpoint_whole_module_optimization
features.description: Code should include a cutpoint command on a whole module which should trigger whole-module optimization removing all contents

metadata.snippet: cutpoint sub_mod

features.name: box_topological_ordering
features.description: Code should include multiple hierarchical boxes where output-to-input dependencies between boxes require correct topological ordering during ABC synthesis. Boxes whose outputs feed other boxes' inputs must be processed first.

features.name: abc_aiger_black_box_mapping
features.description: Code should include black-box module declarations that get mapped to AIGER format for ABC synthesis. ABC requires boxes to have properly declared input/output widths that align with TFI (true fan-in) circuit structure.

features.name: cross_box_wire_dependencies
features.description: Code should include wire connections spanning multiple box boundaries where internal signals of one box fan out to inputs of other boxes. This creates TFI (true fan-in) relationships that ABC topological order must respect.

features.name: synth_ice40_abc_flow
features.description: Code should include SystemVerilog constructs that exercise the synth_ice40 flow with ABC synthesis. The ICE40 architecture uses ABC internally for AIG mapping, which requires proper box ordering.

features.name: bram unused upper bits set to x
features.description: Code should include a block RAM configuration where unused upper bits of a data bus are set to 'x (undefined) instead of '0, using a concatenation with wider bit-width parameter

features.name: single_bit_vector_declaration
features.description: Code should include a single-bit vector declaration using zero-width range [0:0] to distinguish from plain scalar wire.

metadata.snippet: wire [0:0] bar;

features.name: plain_single_bit_wire_declaration
features.description: Code should include a plain single-bit wire declaration without range to distinguish from single-bit vector wire.

metadata.snippet: wire foo;

features.name: right_shift_with_overwidth_constant
features.description: Code should include a right shift operation where the shift amount constant exceeds 32 bits or equals (1<<32)-1, applied to a bit-slice operand narrower than the shift result width.

metadata.snippet: assign out = (in1[1:1] >> 32'hffffffffffff);

features.name: logical_right_shift_large_constant
features.description: Code should include a logical right shift (>>) with a constant shift amount that exceeds 32 bits, triggering integer overflow in opt_expr optimization

metadata.snippet: assign out = signal >> 32'hffffffffffff;

features.name: arithmetic_right_shift_large_constant
features.description: Code should include an arithmetic right shift (>>>) with a constant shift amount that exceeds 32 bits, triggering integer overflow in opt_expr optimization

metadata.snippet:
wire signed w = 1'b1;
assign out = w >>> 36'hfffffffff;

features.name: bit_slice_with_large_shift
features.description: Code should include a bit-slice selection followed by a right shift with a constant shift amount that exceeds 32 bits, triggering integer overflow during optimization

metadata.snippet: assign out = in1[1:1] >> 32'hffffffffffff;

features.name: signed_operand_arithmetic_shift_overflow
features.description: Code should include a signed signal with arithmetic right shift (>>>) using a large constant that overflows 32-bit int, causing incorrect optimization

metadata.snippet:
wire signed s = 1'b1;
assign out = s >>> 36'hfffffffff;

features.name: constant_shift_saturation_safety
features.description: Code should include a right shift with a constant shift amount at the 32-bit boundary (e.g., 0xffffffff), which saturates to INT_MAX and produces zero result

metadata.snippet: assign out = signal >> 32'hffffffff;

features.name: arithmetic_right_shift_large_constant
features.description: Code should include arithmetic right shift (>>>) with a large constant shift amount that may exceed internal bit handling limits, causing incorrect optimization results.

metadata.snippet: wire signed wire_1 = 1'b1; assign out1 = wire_1 >>> 36'hfffffffff;

features.name: explicit_width_hex_constant_shift
features.description: Code should include hex constants with explicit width specification used as shift amounts, which may trigger overflow in internal shift amount processing.

metadata.snippet: assign out1 = wire_1 >>> 36'hfffffffff; assign out2 = wire_1 >>> 36'hfffffff;

features.name: multi_clock_integer_assignment
features.description: Code should include an integer variable assigned in two or more separate always @(posedge clk) blocks, each with a different clock signal.

features.name: cross_clock_domain_scalar_integer
features.description: Code should include a scalar integer variable declared at module scope that is written by processes in different clock domains.

features.name: procedural_shared_integer_multi_always
features.description: Code should include a shared integer modified in multiple procedural blocks triggered by edge-sensitive signals.

features.name: case_item_width_mismatch_triggers_rtlil_assertion
features.description: Code should include a case statement with selector and case item literal widths that do not match, triggering Yosys RTLIL assertion failure in kernel/rtlil.cc during synthesis.

metadata.snippet: case (sel) 3'b001: begin end 3'b010: begin end default: begin end endcase

features.name: constant tri-state assignment to output port
features.description: Code should include a direct assignment of 1'bz to an output port (not inout), where the assignment is a constant tri-state value rather than part of a ternary expression

metadata.snippet:
output my_tri_out;
assign my_tri_out = 1'bz;

features.name: typedef_array_of_typedef_logic
features.description: Code should include a typedef array where the element type is itself a typedef of logic. This pattern triggers recursive typedef resolution during AST cloning, which can cause memory leaks in Yosys when the wiretype attribute is not properly cleaned before assignment.

metadata.snippet: typedef logic T; typedef T [3:0] S;

features.name: typedef_chaining_with_array_dimensions
features.description: Code should include a typedef chain where an intermediate typedef is used as an array type. The pattern requires at least two typedef declarations in sequence where the second uses the first as an array element type, triggering deep clone operations during type resolution.

metadata.snippet: typedef T1 T2; typedef T2 [N:0] T3;

features.name: power_operator_signed_exponent_with_zero_base_x_propagation
features.description: Code should include a power operator (**) where the base is a single-bit signal that can be zero and the exponent is a signed signal that can be negative, triggering IEEE 1364-2005 x-propagation rules when base is zero and exponent is negative.

metadata.snippet:
wire signed [1:0] wire_4;
assign out = (1'b1 ** (zero_base ** wire_4));

features.name: power_operator_nested_with_signed_inner_exponent
features.description: Code should include nested power operators where the inner exponent is signed and can be negative, causing the inner power to evaluate to 'x when the base is zero, which should propagate to the outer result per IEEE 1364-2005.

features.name: wire assignment in clocked always block
features.description: Code should include an output wire being assigned with a non-blocking assignment inside an always @(posedge clk) block

features.name: duplicate_signal_definition_in_scope
features.description: Code should include no duplicate definitions of the same signal name at the same scope level, whether via reg/wire declarations or initial/always procedural blocks. A signal must be defined exactly once per scope to avoid Verilator duplicate-definition errors.

features.name: ifdef_initial_then_reg_definition
features.description: Code should include no case where a signal is initialized in an `ifdef block via initial statement and then re-declared as reg outside that block at the same scope level. This creates conflicting definitions that Verilator rejects.

metadata.snippet:
`ifdef VLOGTB_HIER
  initial begin genclock = 1; end
`endif
reg genclock = 1;  // Conflict: genclock also initialized above

features.name: auto_generated_testbench_clock_declaration
features.description: Code should include a bootstrap clock signal generated via a procedural initial block that is driven unconditionally at time zero. This is a common pattern in auto-generated testbenches where genclock drives simulation timing before the design clock takes over.

features.name: clocked_always_ff_with_combinational_feed
features.description: Code should include an always_ff block with clocked assignments where an internal logic variable is computed combinatorially (assign) and then used in a subsequent clocked always_ff block.

metadata.snippet: logic sum; wire c0; assign c0 = a + b; always_ff @(posedge clk) if (en) sum <= c0;

features.name: conditional_clock_assignment
features.description: Code should include a conditional clocked assignment where a register is updated only when an enable condition is true in an always_ff block.

metadata.snippet: always_ff @(posedge clk) if (enable | rst) reg <= data;

features.name: multi_stage_sequential_logic
features.description: Code should include multi-stage sequential logic where output of one clocked stage feeds into another clocked stage through combinational logic.

metadata.snippet: logic sum; logic sum2; always_ff @(posedge clk) if (spec03) sum <= c0; always_ff @(posedge clk) if (spec02) sum2 <= sum;

features.name: Conditional strip operation in Makefile install target
features.description: Code should include conditional checks before strip operations to allow debug symbol preservation when STRIP variable is empty or undefined.

metadata.snippet: if [ -n "$(STRIP)" ]; then $(INSTALL_SUDO) $(STRIP) -S $(DESTDIR)$(BINDIR)/$(PROGRAM_PREFIX)yosys; fi

features.name: Disable strip by clearing variable when debug is enabled
features.description: Code should include logic to clear STRIP variable when ENABLE_DEBUG is set, ensuring debug symbols are preserved in debug builds.

metadata.snippet:
ifeq ($(ENABLE_DEBUG),1)
CXXFLAGS := -Og -DDEBUG $(filter-out $(OPT_LEVEL),$(CXXFLAGS))
STRIP :=
endif

features.name: port_declaration_order_preservation
features.description: Code should include module ports declared in non-alphabetical order, where input port names are not sorted alphabetically (e.g., A, C, B instead of A, B, C) and output port names are also not sorted (e.g., X, Z, Y instead of X, Y, Z). The backend must preserve the declaration order of named ports through all optimization passes.

features.name: parameterized_module_with_arithmetic_expression_default
features.description: Code should include a module with a parameter declared as 'parameter integer' where the default value is an arithmetic expression rather than a literal constant. This triggers issues in certain synthesis flows (e.g., synth_gowin) that mishandle the $paramod representation of such modules.

features.name: always_ff_with_arithmetic_comparison_in_condition
features.description: Code should include an always_ff block with conditional logic that compares a counter against an arithmetic expression result (e.g., blink_counter < (DIV - 1)). Such comparisons in procedural timing blocks may interact poorly with synthesis optimizations.

features.name: synth_gowin_with_parameterized_top_module
features.description: Code should include a parameterized top-level module being processed by the 'synth_gowin' command. The synth_gowin pass appears to have issues with boxed $paramod modules that use integer parameters with arithmetic expression defaults.

metadata.snippet: yosys -p "synth_gowin -top blinky -json _output.json" blinky.sv

features.name: global_typedef_struct_packed_member_access
features.description: Code should include a typedef struct packed defined at global scope (outside module/package), used as a module port type, with struct member accessed via dot notation inside the module body.

features.name: global_typedef_struct_packed_as_module_io
features.description: Code should include a typedef struct (packed or unpacked) defined at global scope, used as input or output port type of a module that references it.

features.name: single_bit_right_shift_by_oversized_constant
features.description: Code should include a single-bit value being right-shifted by a 32-bit constant that exceeds the operand width

features.name: module with non-alphabetically-ordered input ports
features.description: Code should include a module with input ports declared in non-alphabetical order (X, C, B rather than B, C, X)

features.name: module with non-alphabetically-ordered output ports
features.description: Code should include a module with output ports declared in non-alphabetical order (A, Z, Y rather than A, Y, Z)

features.name: post_decrement_in_expression
features.description: Code should include a post-decrement operator (var--) used within an arithmetic expression inside a procedural block

metadata.snippet: out_diff = (var--) - other_var;

features.name: post_decrement_preserves_original_value
features.description: Code should include a post-decrement operator where the original value is used in the surrounding expression before decrement occurs

metadata.snippet: result = (counter--) - subtrahend;

features.name: variable_reuse_after_post_decrement
features.description: Code should include a post-decrement operator followed by a subsequent statement that reads the same variable to verify the decrement occurred

metadata.snippet: var_m2 = in_val; out_diff = (var_m2--) - sub_val; var_out = var_m2;

features.name: task inout parameter modified in conditional
features.description: Code should include a task with an inout parameter that is modified inside conditional if-else logic

features.name: always_comb block calling task with inout argument
features.description: Code should include an always_comb block that calls a task with an inout argument passed from a local variable

features.name: whitebox_module_in_design_selection
features.description: Code should include a design where a whitebox (boxed module) is part of the selected modules processed by proc_* passes. Whitebox modules are vendor-specific or simulation models kept as black boxes during synthesis, and the proc pass must iterate over all_selected_modules() to properly handle them alongside regular modules.

features.name: abc9_ops_invoking_proc
features.description: Code should include a synthesis flow where abc9_ops calls the proc pass on derived modules containing both regular and boxed (whitebox) modules. The fix requires calling 'proc -noopt' to skip opt_expr, preventing warnings about boxed modules being silently ignored.

features.name: proc_opt_expr_warns_on_boxes
features.description: Code should include a design with boxed modules where the proc pass's default call to opt_expr triggers a warning because opt_expr does not operate on whitebox modules but is called within a context expecting full module coverage. Using 'proc -noopt' bypasses this warning by skipping the opt_expr call entirely.

features.name: all_selected_modules_iteration_pattern
features.description: Code should include the correct iteration pattern over selected modules: using all_selected_modules() to enumerate both regular and boxed modules, rather than iterating only over selected_processes() which silently skips whitebox modules without warning.

features.name: negedge_always_ff_register
features.description: Code should include an always_ff block triggered on negedge clock with non-blocking assignment to a register. This triggers a Yosys optimization bug where the register receives an incorrect (zero) value instead of the assigned literal value.

metadata.snippet:
always_ff @(negedge clk)
    reg_bug <= 7'b0011111;

features.name: negedge_nonblocking_literal_assignment
features.description: Code should include a non-blocking assignment of a multi-bit literal constant (7'b0011111) to a reg variable inside an always_ff block triggered by negedge clock. This exposes a synthesis optimization bug where the literal value is corrupted to zero.

features.name: negedge_clock_initial_high
features.description: Code should include a clock signal initialized to 1 and toggled via procedural assignment in an initial block to trigger a negedge event. This combined with always_ff negedge trigger exposes the Yosys bug.

metadata.snippet:
reg clk = 1;
initial begin
    #1 clk = 0;  // negedge trigger
end

features.name: dff_with_const_undefined_input
features.description: Code should include a DFF cell with a constant undefined (x) value as its D input, which triggers removal optimization in opt_dff when the output feeds into another DFF

metadata.snippet:
cell $dff \dffb
    parameter \CLK_POLARITY 1
    parameter \WIDTH 1
    connect \CLK \clk
    connect \D 1'x
    connect \Q \qb
  end

features.name: dff_chain_with_feedback_wire
features.description: Code should include a chain of two DFFs where the first DFF's D input is the Q output of the second DFF, creating a feedback pattern that causes opt_dff to remove the second DFF while ModWalker still holds references

metadata.snippet:
cell $dff \dffa
    connect \D \qb
    connect \Q \qa
  end
  cell $dff \dffb

features.description: Code should include a combinational case statement where the selector covers all contiguous values from 0 to 2^n-1, creating a dense parallel multiplexer pattern suitable for shift-based optimization

features.name: sparse case statement with non-contiguous selector values
features.description: Code should include a combinational case statement where the selector values are non-contiguous with large gaps, creating a sparse parallel multiplexer that requires offset subtraction and bit manipulation for shift-based conversion

features.name: multiple case statements driving same output in combinational block
features.description: Code should include two or more combinational case statements within the same always block that both assign to the same output variable, exercising last-assignment-wins semantics and multi-mux merging optimization

features.name: whitebox attribute on module
features.description: Code should include a module with the (* whitebox *) attribute, which affects how tools like Yosys preserve module contents during blackbox creation

features.name: lib_whitebox attribute on module
features.description: Code should include a module with the (* lib_whitebox *) attribute, a library-specific variant of whitebox that controls preservation during -lib reads

features.name: blackbox attribute on empty module
features.description: Code should include an empty module with the (* blackbox *) attribute, which marks it as having no implementation

features.name: combined whitebox and lib_whitebox attributes with value
features.description: Code should include a module with combined attribute syntax (* whitebox, lib_whitebox = 0 *), demonstrating multi-attribute declaration with values

features.name: specify block with full connection timing path
features.description: Code should include a specify block containing a full connection timing path using the *> operator between source and destination signals

features.name: conditional timing path with if in specify block
features.description: Code should include a specify block with a conditional timing path using an if guard controlling when the timing annotation applies

features.name: specify data destination annotation with colon operator
features.description: Code should include a specify block timing path with data destination annotation using the colon operator to associate a data signal with the destination

features.name: timing values with min typ max triple notation
features.description: Code should include timing values expressed in min:typ:max triple format for rise and fall delays in a specify block

features.name: wire with negative range index
features.description: Code should include a wire declaration where the range left bound is a negative integer, producing an invalid or unusual width specification that the frontend must validate

metadata.snippet: wire [-2:0] a;

features.name: ascending range with negative left bound
features.description: Code should include a net or variable declaration with an ascending range (left < right) where the left bound is negative, exercising edge cases in range validation logic

metadata.snippet: wire [-2:0] a;

features.name: reduction operator on zero-width signal
features.description: Code should include a reduction OR (or reduce_bool) operation applied to a zero-width signal, which logically evaluates to 0 but may produce invalid Verilog output such as |{ } with an empty concatenation

metadata.snippet:
wire _0_;
assign _0_ = | {  };

features.name: empty concatenation list in expression
features.description: Code should include an empty concatenation {} used as an operand, which is syntactically illegal in Verilog (IEEE 1364-2005 A.8.1) and can be produced by tools when emitting zero-width signal expressions

metadata.snippet: assign _0_ = |{  };

features.name: cell with zero-width input parameter
features.description: Code should include a cell (e.g. reduce_bool) where the A_WIDTH parameter is set to 0 and the input connection is empty, representing a zero-width signal fed into an operator

metadata.snippet:
cell $reduce_bool $2
  parameter \A_SIGNED 0
  parameter \A_WIDTH 0
  parameter \Y_WIDTH 1
  connect \A {}
  connect \Y $1
end

features.name: combinational case with default assignment as full mux
features.description: Code should include a combinational always block where a default assignment precedes a case statement that covers all selector values, forming a complete multiplexer pattern

features.name: multi-way case selection with 2-bit selector
features.description: Code should include a case statement with a multi-bit selector choosing among four or more data inputs of matching width

features.name: preceding default value before case in combinational block
features.description: Code should include a default value assignment before a case statement in a combinational always block, where the case does not include a default branch

features.name: multiple assign drivers on same net
features.description: Code should include two or more continuous assign statements driving the same net, creating conflicting drivers that stress optimizer passes

metadata.snippet:
assign c = ^K;
assign c = 9 ^ k;

features.name: inout port with conditional initial assignment
features.description: Code should include an inout port declared with a conditional expression as its initial value, referencing a forward-declared or undeclared signal

metadata.snippet: inout b = 0 == c;

features.name: reduction xor on implicitly declared wire
features.description: Code should include a unary reduction XOR operator applied to an implicitly declared (undeclared) wire identifier

metadata.snippet:
wire c;
assign c = ^K;

features.name: integer declaration with defparam-like suffix
features.description: Code should include an integer variable declaration followed by a hash suffix (#0), which exercises edge cases in the parser and downstream passes

metadata.snippet: integer c#0;

features.name: bitwise xor with literal and implicit wire
features.description: Code should include a bitwise XOR between a numeric literal and an implicitly declared wire, combined with other drivers on the same target net

metadata.snippet: assign c = 9 ^ k;

features.name: bit-slice multiplication producing narrow result
features.description: Code should include a multiplication of two bit slices from the same signal, with the result width equal to one of the operand widths, causing potential truncation of the product

features.name: partial product of same-signal slices
features.description: Code should include a multiplication where both operands are bit-selected sub-ranges of the same bus signal, which can expose optimizer assumptions about signal independence

features.name: narrow multiply with truncated result
features.description: Code should include a multiply of two 4-bit values assigned to a 4-bit output, where the full product requires 8 bits and the upper bits are implicitly truncated

metadata.snippet:
wire [3:0] a, b;
wire [3:0] out = a * b;

features.name: wide constant right shift by variable
features.description: Code should include a right-shift operation where a large-width (256-bit) constant literal is shifted by a variable operand of narrower width (8-bit)

metadata.snippet:
input [7:0] addr;
wire [255:0] shifted = 256'haaaa0000aaaa0000aaaa0000aaaa0000aaaa0000aaaa0000aaaa0000aaaa0000 >> addr;

features.name: bit-select from wide shift expression
features.description: Code should include a single-bit output driven by a bit-select (index 0) of a wide shift expression result, testing whether synthesis preserves correct bit-level semantics through optimization passes

features.name: initial block on multi-bit register with partial bit usage
features.description: Code should include a multi-bit register with an initial value assignment, where only a subrange of bits is used in downstream logic, leaving some bits unused and subject to optimization removal

features.name: bit-range selection from wider register in combinational logic
features.description: Code should include a combinational always block that reads only a subset of bits from a wider register, creating unused MSB bits that synthesizers may optimize away

features.name: initial value on register with sequential and combinational consumers
features.description: Code should include a register that is initialized via an initial block, updated in a sequential always block (posedge clk), and read in a separate combinational always block

features.name: integer inout port
features.description: Code should include an integer-typed port declared as inout, which creates a 32-bit bidirectional signal that can interact with both continuous assignments and procedural blocks

metadata.snippet: integer inout b;

features.name: bitwise AND with constant zero on wide signal
features.description: Code should include a bitwise AND operation between an undeclared/wide signal and the constant zero, which produces a cell that the optimizer may try to reduce to a single bit

metadata.snippet:
always
if(8) b = M & 0;

features.name: assignment to constant bit selection
features.description: Code should include a continuous assignment targeting a constant or bit-select of zero, where the LHS is a constant expression that should normally be invalid

metadata.snippet:
assign &0 = 0;
assign 0 = 0;

features.name: always block with conditional on constant and implicit declarations
features.description: Code should include an always block with an if-statement whose condition is a non-zero constant, assigning to an implicitly declared identifier, triggering creation of wires with no drivers

metadata.snippet:
always
if(8) b = _;

features.name: deeply nested cascaded if-else-if chain
features.description: Code should include a deeply nested cascaded if-else-if chain with many branches (30+) inside an always block, where each branch compares a selector against a constant and assigns a different value to the output

features.name: parameterized indexed part-select with multiplier offset
features.description: Code should include indexed part-select operations using the +: operator where the base index is computed as a parameterized expression involving multiplication, e.g. i[k*W+:W]

features.name: module with $clog2 in port declaration
features.description: Code should include a module whose port width is derived from the $clog2 system function applied to a parameter

metadata.snippet:
parameter N=33;
input [$clog2(N)-1:0] s;

features.name: replication in default branch of if-else
features.description: Code should include a replication operator used in the default else branch of a cascaded if-else chain, producing an X-valued output of parameterized width

metadata.snippet:
parameter W=8;
else o <= {W{1'bx}};

features.name: dynamic indexed part-select with non-power-of-2 width
features.description: Code should include a dynamic indexed part-select (+:) where the slice width is a non-power-of-2 parameter value, used on a packed array whose total size is the product of two parameters

features.name: clog2 in port width declaration
features.description: Code should include a port declaration using $clog2 of a parameter to compute the selector bit width

metadata.snippet:
parameter N=8;
input [$clog2(N)-1:0] s;

features.name: parameterized packed array with product dimension
features.description: Code should include a packed array whose width is computed as the product of two parameters (N*W-1:0)

metadata.snippet:
parameter N=8, W=3;
input [N*W-1:0] i;

features.name: mux inferred from indexed part-select of wide array
features.description: Code should include a continuous assignment that extracts a variable-width slice from a wide packed array using a dynamic base index, which infers a multiplexer structure

features.name: for-loop variable persistence after loop exit
features.description: Code should include a for-loop with an integer loop variable whose value is read after the loop terminates within the same always block, verifying that the variable retains its last assigned value per Verilog semantics

features.name: integer variable dual use as loop iterator and array index
features.description: Code should include an integer variable used both as a for-loop iteration counter and subsequently as an array index expression after the loop body, testing that the post-loop index value resolves correctly

features.name: dynamic part-select with variable base expression
features.description: Code should include a dynamic part-select using the +: operator where the base index is a variable expression computed from multiplication of a select signal and a width parameter

features.name: clog2 in port width declaration
features.description: Code should include use of the $clog2 system function to compute the bit width of a select signal in a port declaration

metadata.snippet:
parameter N=3;
input [$clog2(N)-1:0] s;

features.name: parameterized array width with multiplication expression
features.description: Code should include a packed array whose width is computed as a multiplication of two parameters

metadata.snippet:
parameter N=3, W=3;
input [N*W-1:0] i;

features.name: integer loop variable in for-loop unrolling
features.description: Code should include a for-loop with an explicitly declared integer variable as the loop iterator, which the synthesis tool must unroll

features.name: post-loop reference to loop variable
features.description: Code should include a reference to the loop variable after the for-loop exits, where the variable should hold its final value that failed the loop condition

features.name: LHS concatenation of bit slices from same 2D array element
features.description: Code should include a concatenation on the left-hand side of an assignment where the concatenated parts are adjacent bit slices of the same 2D unpacked array element, such as {arr[i][j+1], arr[i][j]} = expr

metadata.snippet:
reg [DIM_1-1:0] mem [DIM_2-1:0];
{mem[i][j+1], mem[i][j]} = mem[i][j] + mem[i+1][j];

features.name: parameterized 2D memory array with multiplied port width
features.description: Code should include a 2D unpacked array (memory) where the port width is the product of two parameters (DIM_1*DIM_2), and the memory is indexed with parameter-based part-select using +: operator

features.name: nested for loops with non-unit stride on array indices
features.description: Code should include nested for-loops iterating over a 2D unpacked array where the outer loop uses a non-unit stride (e.g., i=i+3) and the inner loop iterates over bit indices of each array element

metadata.snippet:
for (i = 0; i < DIM_2-1; i=i+3) begin
  for (j = 0; j < DIM_1-1; j=j+1) begin
    mem[i][j+1] = mem[i][j] + mem[i+1][j];
  end
end

features.name: array copy between two 2D memories in combinational block
features.description: Code should include a combinational always block that copies elements from one 2D unpacked array to another element-by-element using a for loop, combined with a second block performing array-to-vector packing

features.name: function with local parameter referencing module parameter
features.description: Code should include a function declaration that contains a local parameter whose initializer expression references a parameter from the enclosing module scope

features.name: wide register with explicit initializer in LFSR shift pattern
features.description: Code should include a multi-bit register (width > 16) explicitly initialized to a known value (e.g. zero), used in a shift register feedback loop where the shift-and-feedback logic is applied every clock edge

features.name: concatenation-based shift with multi-tap XOR/XNOR feedback
features.description: Code should include a non-blocking assignment using concatenation to perform a shift operation, where the inserted bit is computed from multiple taps of the same register combined with XNOR operators

metadata.snippet: state <= { state[23:1], state[24] ~^ state[23] ~^ state[22] ~^ state[17] };

features.name: register init value propagation across sequential optimization boundary
features.description: Code should include a register with a known binary initializer where the register feeds a shift-chain, so that synthesis tools must propagate the init attribute through shift-register inference optimization passes

features.name: elaboration system task in generate if block
features.description: Code should include an elaboration system task ($info or $warning) inside a generate if block that checks a parameter value at elaboration time

features.name: elaboration error task halting synthesis
features.description: Code should include an $error or $fatal elaboration system task inside a generate block that stops synthesis for illegal parameter combinations

features.name: elaboration system task in generate for loop
features.description: Code should include an elaboration system task inside a generate for loop to validate conditions during elaboration iteration

metadata.snippet:
genvar i;
generate
  for (i = 0; i < 4; i = i + 1) begin
    if (i == 3) begin
      $info("Reached last iteration");
    end
  end
endgenerate

features.name: elaboration system task in generate case block
features.description: Code should include an elaboration system task inside a generate case block to report warnings or errors for specific parameter values

metadata.snippet:
parameter OP = "ADD";
generate
  case (OP)
    "ADD": begin end
    "SUB": begin end
    default: begin
      $warning("Unknown operation");
    end
  endcase
endgenerate

features.name: deep generate loop with internal cell instantiation
features.description: Code should include a generate for-loop that instantiates internal Yosys cell primitives (e.g. $_DFFE_PP_) in a long chain of depth 100 or more, exercising read_verilog -icells and opt_clean scalability

features.name: parameterized shift register with dynamic bit select
features.description: Code should include a parameterized register array used as a shift register where the output is selected dynamically via a variable index into the shift register state, creating variable-length shift register behavior

features.name: generate loop with initial blocks on register array elements
features.description: Code should include a generate for-loop that applies initial blocks to individual elements of a multi-dimensional register array, producing many initial processes that stress synthesis tools

features.name: nested generate for-loops with parameterized large depth
features.description: Code should include nested generate for-loops iterating over parameterized dimensions where depth values exceed 100, causing quadratic explosion in generated instances that stress opt_clean and synthesis passes

features.name: var port kind keyword in output port declaration
features.description: Code should include an output port declared with the explicit 'var' port kind keyword, as in 'output var', which specifies the port as a variable rather than a net per IEEE 1800-2012 section 23.2.2.3

metadata.snippet: output var a

features.name: always_comb driving output var port
features.description: Code should include an always_comb block that drives an output variable port, combining the 'var' port kind with combinational procedural assignment

metadata.snippet:
output var a, input b;
always_comb a = b;

features.name: non-ANSI port declaration with keyword as port name
features.description: Code should include a module with non-ANSI style port declaration where a Verilog keyword (e.g. 'output') is used as a port identifier name

features.name: function with local parameter shadowing module parameter
features.description: Code should include a module-level parameter and a function that declares a local parameter with the same name to shadow the module-level one

features.name: function parameter expression referencing module-level parameter
features.description: Code should include a function that declares a local parameter whose value is an expression referencing the module-level parameter

features.name: function referencing module parameter directly in body
features.description: Code should include a function that uses the module-level parameter directly in its body without declaring a local copy

features.name: comparison result assigned to multi-bit register
features.description: Code should include a comparison expression (==) whose 1-bit result is stored in a multi-bit register wider than 1 bit

features.name: part-select of register used as shift amount
features.description: Code should include a single-bit part-select (e.g. [1:1]) on a multi-bit register that is then used as the shift amount in a shift-right operation

features.name: constant shifted by part-select of comparison result register
features.description: Code should include a shift-right of a constant literal by a part-select of a register that was assigned a comparison result, creating a dataflow chain from comparison through part-select into shift

features.name: register with explicit initial value in synchronous always block
features.description: Code should include a register declared with an explicit initial value that is assigned inside a clocked always block, testing whether the synthesis tool preserves the initial value

features.name: signed input wire driving register assignment
features.description: Code should include a signed input wire that drives a register assignment in a synchronous block, testing signed-to-unsigned propagation across the register boundary

features.name: parameterized multi-bit shift register
features.description: Code should include a multi-bit register with parameterized width used in a shift register feedback pattern where the register value is concatenated with a single-bit input and assigned back to itself

features.name: MSB bit-select output from multi-bit register
features.description: Code should include a continuous assignment that extracts a single bit (MSB) from a parameterized-width register as an output

features.name: register with parameterized width range declaration
features.description: Code should include a register declared with a width derived from a parameter using a descending bit range expression

metadata.snippet:
parameter DEPTH = 10;
reg [DEPTH-1:0] regs;
